schemaClasses.name: AMRadioChannel
schemaClasses.label: AMRadioChannel
schemaClasses.description: A radio channel that uses AM.

schemaClasses.name: APIReference
schemaClasses.label: APIReference
schemaClasses.description: Reference documentation for application programming interfaces (APIs).

schemaClasses.name: AboutPage
schemaClasses.label: AboutPage
schemaClasses.description: Web page type: About page.

schemaClasses.name: AcceptAction
schemaClasses.label: AcceptAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of committing to/adopting an object.

Related actions:

* [[RejectAction]]: The antonym of AcceptAction.

schemaClasses.name: Accommodation
schemaClasses.label: Accommodation
schemaClasses.description:
An accommodation is a place that can accommodate human beings, e.g. a hotel room, a camping pitch, or a meeting room. Many accommodations are for overnight stays, but this is not a mandatory requirement. For more specific types of accommodations not defined in schema.org, one can use [[additionalType]] with external vocabularies.

See also the dedicated document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations.

schemaClasses.name: AccountingService
schemaClasses.label: AccountingService
schemaClasses.description: Accountancy business.\n\nAs a [[LocalBusiness]] it can be described as a [[provider]] of one or more [[Service]]\(s).

schemaClasses.name: AchieveAction
schemaClasses.label: AchieveAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of accomplishing something via previous efforts. It is an instantaneous action rather than an ongoing process.

schemaClasses.name: Action
schemaClasses.label: Action
schemaClasses.description: An action performed by a direct agent and indirect participants upon a direct object. Optionally happens at a location with the help of an inanimate instrument. The execution of the action may produce a result. Specific action sub-type documentation specifies the exact expectation of each argument/role.\n\nSee also [blog post](https://blog.schema.org/2014/04/16/announcing-schema-org-actions/) and [Actions overview document](https://schema.org/docs/actions.html).

schemaClasses.name: ActionAccessSpecification
schemaClasses.label: ActionAccessSpecification
schemaClasses.description: A set of requirements that must be fulfilled in order to perform an Action.

schemaClasses.name: ActionStatusType
schemaClasses.label: ActionStatusType
schemaClasses.description: The status of an Action.

schemaClasses.name: ActivateAction
schemaClasses.label: ActivateAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of starting or activating a device or application (e.g. starting a timer or turning on a flashlight).

schemaClasses.name: AddAction
schemaClasses.label: AddAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of editing by adding an object to a collection.

schemaClasses.name: AdministrativeArea
schemaClasses.label: AdministrativeArea
schemaClasses.description: A geographical region, typically under the jurisdiction of a particular government.

schemaClasses.name: AdultEntertainment
schemaClasses.label: AdultEntertainment
schemaClasses.description: An adult entertainment establishment.

schemaClasses.name: AdultOrientedEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: AdultOrientedEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Enumeration of considerations that make a product relevant or potentially restricted for adults only.

schemaClasses.name: AdvertiserContentArticle
schemaClasses.label: AdvertiserContentArticle
schemaClasses.description: An [[Article]] that an external entity has paid to place or to produce to its specifications. Includes [advertorials](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Advertorial), sponsored content, native advertising and other paid content.

schemaClasses.name: AggregateOffer
schemaClasses.label: AggregateOffer
schemaClasses.description: When a single product is associated with multiple offers (for example, the same pair of shoes is offered by different merchants), then AggregateOffer can be used.\n\nNote: AggregateOffers are normally expected to associate multiple offers that all share the same defined [[businessFunction]] value, or default to http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Sell if businessFunction is not explicitly defined.

schemaClasses.name: AggregateRating
schemaClasses.label: AggregateRating
schemaClasses.description: The average rating based on multiple ratings or reviews.

schemaClasses.name: AgreeAction
schemaClasses.label: AgreeAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of expressing a consistency of opinion with the object. An agent agrees to/about an object (a proposition, topic or theme) with participants.

schemaClasses.name: Airline
schemaClasses.label: Airline
schemaClasses.description: An organization that provides flights for passengers.

schemaClasses.name: Airport
schemaClasses.label: Airport
schemaClasses.description: An airport.

schemaClasses.name: AlignmentObject
schemaClasses.label: AlignmentObject
schemaClasses.description:
An intangible item that describes an alignment between a learning resource and a node in an educational framework.
Should not be used where the nature of the alignment can be described using a simple property, for example to express that a resource [[teaches]] or [[assesses]] a competency.

schemaClasses.name: AllocateAction
schemaClasses.label: AllocateAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of organizing tasks/objects/events by associating resources to it.

schemaClasses.name: AmpStory
schemaClasses.label: AmpStory
schemaClasses.description: A creative work with a visual storytelling format intended to be viewed online, particularly on mobile devices.

schemaClasses.name: AmusementPark
schemaClasses.label: AmusementPark
schemaClasses.description: An amusement park.

schemaClasses.name: AnalysisNewsArticle
schemaClasses.label: AnalysisNewsArticle
schemaClasses.description: An AnalysisNewsArticle is a [[NewsArticle]] that, while based on factual reporting, incorporates the expertise of the author/producer, offering interpretations and conclusions.

schemaClasses.name: AnatomicalStructure
schemaClasses.label: AnatomicalStructure
schemaClasses.description: Any part of the human body, typically a component of an anatomical system. Organs, tissues, and cells are all anatomical structures.

schemaClasses.name: AnatomicalSystem
schemaClasses.label: AnatomicalSystem
schemaClasses.description: An anatomical system is a group of anatomical structures that work together to perform a certain task. Anatomical systems, such as organ systems, are one organizing principle of anatomy, and can include circulatory, digestive, endocrine, integumentary, immune, lymphatic, muscular, nervous, reproductive, respiratory, skeletal, urinary, vestibular, and other systems.

schemaClasses.name: AnimalShelter
schemaClasses.label: AnimalShelter
schemaClasses.description: Animal shelter.

schemaClasses.name: Answer
schemaClasses.label: Answer
schemaClasses.description: An answer offered to a question; perhaps correct, perhaps opinionated or wrong.

schemaClasses.name: Apartment
schemaClasses.label: Apartment
schemaClasses.description: An apartment (in American English) or flat (in British English) is a self-contained housing unit (a type of residential real estate) that occupies only part of a building (source: Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia, see http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apartment).

schemaClasses.name: ApartmentComplex
schemaClasses.label: ApartmentComplex
schemaClasses.description: Residence type: Apartment complex.

schemaClasses.name: AppendAction
schemaClasses.label: AppendAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of inserting at the end if an ordered collection.

schemaClasses.name: ApplyAction
schemaClasses.label: ApplyAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of registering to an organization/service without the guarantee to receive it.

Related actions:

* [[RegisterAction]]: Unlike RegisterAction, ApplyAction has no guarantees that the application will be accepted.

schemaClasses.name: ApprovedIndication
schemaClasses.label: ApprovedIndication
schemaClasses.description: An indication for a medical therapy that has been formally specified or approved by a regulatory body that regulates use of the therapy; for example, the US FDA approves indications for most drugs in the US.

schemaClasses.name: Aquarium
schemaClasses.label: Aquarium
schemaClasses.description: Aquarium.

schemaClasses.name: ArchiveComponent
schemaClasses.label: ArchiveComponent
schemaClasses.description: An intangible type to be applied to any archive content, carrying with it a set of properties required to describe archival items and collections.

schemaClasses.name: ArchiveOrganization
schemaClasses.label: ArchiveOrganization
schemaClasses.description: An organization with archival holdings. An organization which keeps and preserves archival material and typically makes it accessible to the public.

schemaClasses.name: ArriveAction
schemaClasses.label: ArriveAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of arriving at a place. An agent arrives at a destination from a fromLocation, optionally with participants.

schemaClasses.name: ArtGallery
schemaClasses.label: ArtGallery
schemaClasses.description: An art gallery.

schemaClasses.name: Artery
schemaClasses.label: Artery
schemaClasses.description: A type of blood vessel that specifically carries blood away from the heart.

schemaClasses.name: Article
schemaClasses.label: Article
schemaClasses.description: An article, such as a news article or piece of investigative report. Newspapers and magazines have articles of many different types and this is intended to cover them all.\n\nSee also [blog post](https://blog.schema.org/2014/09/02/schema-org-support-for-bibliographic-relationships-and-periodicals/).

schemaClasses.name: AskAction
schemaClasses.label: AskAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of posing a question / favor to someone.

Related actions:

* [[ReplyAction]]: Appears generally as a response to AskAction.

schemaClasses.name: AskPublicNewsArticle
schemaClasses.label: AskPublicNewsArticle
schemaClasses.description: A [[NewsArticle]] expressing an open call by a [[NewsMediaOrganization]] asking the public for input, insights, clarifications, anecdotes, documentation, etc., on an issue, for reporting purposes.

schemaClasses.name: AssessAction
schemaClasses.label: AssessAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of forming one's opinion, reaction or sentiment.

schemaClasses.name: AssignAction
schemaClasses.label: AssignAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of allocating an action/event/task to some destination (someone or something).

schemaClasses.name: Atlas
schemaClasses.label: Atlas
schemaClasses.description: A collection or bound volume of maps, charts, plates or tables, physical or in media form illustrating any subject.

schemaClasses.name: Attorney
schemaClasses.label: Attorney
schemaClasses.description: Professional service: Attorney. \n\nThis type is deprecated - [[LegalService]] is more inclusive and less ambiguous.

schemaClasses.name: Audience
schemaClasses.label: Audience
schemaClasses.description: Intended audience for an item, i.e. the group for whom the item was created.

schemaClasses.name: AudioObject
schemaClasses.label: AudioObject
schemaClasses.description: An audio file.

schemaClasses.name: AudioObjectSnapshot
schemaClasses.label: AudioObjectSnapshot
schemaClasses.description: A specific and exact (byte-for-byte) version of an [[AudioObject]]. Two byte-for-byte identical files, for the purposes of this type, considered identical. If they have different embedded metadata the files will differ. Different external facts about the files, e.g. creator or dateCreated that aren't represented in their actual content, do not affect this notion of identity.

schemaClasses.name: Audiobook
schemaClasses.label: Audiobook
schemaClasses.description: An audiobook.

schemaClasses.name: AuthenticateAction
schemaClasses.label: AuthenticateAction
schemaClasses.description: The action of authenticating into a device or application.

schemaClasses.name: AuthorizeAction
schemaClasses.label: AuthorizeAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of granting permission to an object.

schemaClasses.name: AutoBodyShop
schemaClasses.label: AutoBodyShop
schemaClasses.description: Auto body shop.

schemaClasses.name: AutoDealer
schemaClasses.label: AutoDealer
schemaClasses.description: An car dealership.

schemaClasses.name: AutoPartsStore
schemaClasses.label: AutoPartsStore
schemaClasses.description: An auto parts store.

schemaClasses.name: AutoRental
schemaClasses.label: AutoRental
schemaClasses.description: A car rental business.

schemaClasses.name: AutoRepair
schemaClasses.label: AutoRepair
schemaClasses.description: Car repair business.

schemaClasses.name: AutoWash
schemaClasses.label: AutoWash
schemaClasses.description: A car wash business.

schemaClasses.name: AutomatedTeller
schemaClasses.label: AutomatedTeller
schemaClasses.description: ATM/cash machine.

schemaClasses.name: AutomotiveBusiness
schemaClasses.label: AutomotiveBusiness
schemaClasses.description: Car repair, sales, or parts.

schemaClasses.name: BackgroundNewsArticle
schemaClasses.label: BackgroundNewsArticle
schemaClasses.description: A [[NewsArticle]] providing historical context, definition and detail on a specific topic (aka "explainer" or "backgrounder"). For example, an in-depth article or frequently-asked-questions ([FAQ](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FAQ)) document on topics such as Climate Change or the European Union. Other kinds of background material from a non-news setting are often described using [[Book]] or [[Article]], in particular [[ScholarlyArticle]]. See also [[NewsArticle]] for related vocabulary from a learning/education perspective.

schemaClasses.name: Bakery
schemaClasses.label: Bakery
schemaClasses.description: A bakery.

schemaClasses.name: BankAccount
schemaClasses.label: BankAccount
schemaClasses.description: A product or service offered by a bank whereby one may deposit, withdraw or transfer money and in some cases be paid interest.

schemaClasses.name: BankOrCreditUnion
schemaClasses.label: BankOrCreditUnion
schemaClasses.description: Bank or credit union.

schemaClasses.name: BarOrPub
schemaClasses.label: BarOrPub
schemaClasses.description: A bar or pub.

schemaClasses.name: Barcode
schemaClasses.label: Barcode
schemaClasses.description: An image of a visual machine-readable code such as a barcode or QR code.

schemaClasses.name: Beach
schemaClasses.label: Beach
schemaClasses.description: Beach.

schemaClasses.name: BeautySalon
schemaClasses.label: BeautySalon
schemaClasses.description: Beauty salon.

schemaClasses.name: BedAndBreakfast
schemaClasses.label: BedAndBreakfast
schemaClasses.description:
Bed and breakfast.

See also the dedicated document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations.

schemaClasses.name: BedDetails
schemaClasses.label: BedDetails
schemaClasses.description: An entity holding detailed information about the available bed types, e.g. the quantity of twin beds for a hotel room. For the single case of just one bed of a certain type, you can use bed directly with a text. See also [[BedType]] (under development).

schemaClasses.name: BedType
schemaClasses.label: BedType
schemaClasses.description: A type of bed. This is used for indicating the bed or beds available in an accommodation.

schemaClasses.name: BefriendAction
schemaClasses.label: BefriendAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of forming a personal connection with someone (object) mutually/bidirectionally/symmetrically.

Related actions:

* [[FollowAction]]: Unlike FollowAction, BefriendAction implies that the connection is reciprocal.

schemaClasses.name: BikeStore
schemaClasses.label: BikeStore
schemaClasses.description: A bike store.

schemaClasses.name: BioChemEntity
schemaClasses.label: BioChemEntity
schemaClasses.description: Any biological, chemical, or biochemical thing. For example: a protein; a gene; a chemical; a synthetic chemical.

schemaClasses.name: Blog
schemaClasses.label: Blog
schemaClasses.description: A [blog](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blog), sometimes known as a "weblog". Note that the individual posts ([[BlogPosting]]s) in a [[Blog]] are often colloquially referred to by the same term.

schemaClasses.name: BlogPosting
schemaClasses.label: BlogPosting
schemaClasses.description: A blog post.

schemaClasses.name: BloodTest
schemaClasses.label: BloodTest
schemaClasses.description: A medical test performed on a sample of a patient's blood.

schemaClasses.name: BoardingPolicyType
schemaClasses.label: BoardingPolicyType
schemaClasses.description: A type of boarding policy used by an airline.

schemaClasses.name: BoatReservation
schemaClasses.label: BoatReservation
schemaClasses.description:
A reservation for boat travel.

Note: This type is for information about actual reservations, e.g. in confirmation emails or HTML pages with individual confirmations of reservations. For offers of tickets, use [[Offer]].

schemaClasses.name: BoatTerminal
schemaClasses.label: BoatTerminal
schemaClasses.description: A terminal for boats, ships, and other water vessels.

schemaClasses.name: BoatTrip
schemaClasses.label: BoatTrip
schemaClasses.description: A trip on a commercial ferry line.

schemaClasses.name: BodyMeasurementTypeEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: BodyMeasurementTypeEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Enumerates types (or dimensions) of a person's body measurements, for example for fitting of clothes.

schemaClasses.name: BodyOfWater
schemaClasses.label: BodyOfWater
schemaClasses.description: A body of water, such as a sea, ocean, or lake.

schemaClasses.name: Bone
schemaClasses.label: Bone
schemaClasses.description: Rigid connective tissue that comprises up the skeletal structure of the human body.

schemaClasses.name: Book
schemaClasses.label: Book
schemaClasses.description: A book.

schemaClasses.name: BookFormatType
schemaClasses.label: BookFormatType
schemaClasses.description: The publication format of the book.

schemaClasses.name: BookSeries
schemaClasses.label: BookSeries
schemaClasses.description: A series of books. Included books can be indicated with the hasPart property.

schemaClasses.name: BookStore
schemaClasses.label: BookStore
schemaClasses.description: A bookstore.

schemaClasses.name: BookmarkAction
schemaClasses.label: BookmarkAction
schemaClasses.description: An agent bookmarks/flags/labels/tags/marks an object.

schemaClasses.name: Boolean
schemaClasses.label: Boolean
schemaClasses.description: Boolean: True or False.

schemaClasses.name: BorrowAction
schemaClasses.label: BorrowAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of obtaining an object under an agreement to return it at a later date. Reciprocal of LendAction.

Related actions:

* [[LendAction]]: Reciprocal of BorrowAction.

schemaClasses.name: BowlingAlley
schemaClasses.label: BowlingAlley
schemaClasses.description: A bowling alley.

schemaClasses.name: BrainStructure
schemaClasses.label: BrainStructure
schemaClasses.description: Any anatomical structure which pertains to the soft nervous tissue functioning as the coordinating center of sensation and intellectual and nervous activity.

schemaClasses.name: Brand
schemaClasses.label: Brand
schemaClasses.description: A brand is a name used by an organization or business person for labeling a product, product group, or similar.

schemaClasses.name: BreadcrumbList
schemaClasses.label: BreadcrumbList
schemaClasses.description: A BreadcrumbList is an ItemList consisting of a chain of linked Web pages, typically described using at least their URL and their name, and typically ending with the current page.\n\nThe [[position]] property is used to reconstruct the order of the items in a BreadcrumbList. The convention is that a breadcrumb list has an [[itemListOrder]] of [[ItemListOrderAscending]] (lower values listed first), and that the first items in this list correspond to the "top" or beginning of the breadcrumb trail, e.g. with a site or section homepage. The specific values of 'position' are not assigned meaning for a BreadcrumbList, but they should be integers, e.g. beginning with '1' for the first item in the list.

schemaClasses.name: Brewery
schemaClasses.label: Brewery
schemaClasses.description: Brewery.

schemaClasses.name: Bridge
schemaClasses.label: Bridge
schemaClasses.description: A bridge.

schemaClasses.name: BroadcastChannel
schemaClasses.label: BroadcastChannel
schemaClasses.description: A unique instance of a BroadcastService on a CableOrSatelliteService lineup.

schemaClasses.name: BroadcastEvent
schemaClasses.label: BroadcastEvent
schemaClasses.description: An over the air or online broadcast event.

schemaClasses.name: BroadcastFrequencySpecification
schemaClasses.label: BroadcastFrequencySpecification
schemaClasses.description: The frequency in MHz and the modulation used for a particular BroadcastService.

schemaClasses.name: BroadcastService
schemaClasses.label: BroadcastService
schemaClasses.description: A delivery service through which content is provided via broadcast over the air or online.

schemaClasses.name: BrokerageAccount
schemaClasses.label: BrokerageAccount
schemaClasses.description: An account that allows an investor to deposit funds and place investment orders with a licensed broker or brokerage firm.

schemaClasses.name: BuddhistTemple
schemaClasses.label: BuddhistTemple
schemaClasses.description: A Buddhist temple.

schemaClasses.name: BusOrCoach
schemaClasses.label: BusOrCoach
schemaClasses.description: A bus (also omnibus or autobus) is a road vehicle designed to carry passengers. Coaches are luxury buses, usually in service for long distance travel.

schemaClasses.name: BusReservation
schemaClasses.label: BusReservation
schemaClasses.description: A reservation for bus travel. \n\nNote: This type is for information about actual reservations, e.g. in confirmation emails or HTML pages with individual confirmations of reservations. For offers of tickets, use [[Offer]].

schemaClasses.name: BusStation
schemaClasses.label: BusStation
schemaClasses.description: A bus station.

schemaClasses.name: BusStop
schemaClasses.label: BusStop
schemaClasses.description: A bus stop.

schemaClasses.name: BusTrip
schemaClasses.label: BusTrip
schemaClasses.description: A trip on a commercial bus line.

schemaClasses.name: BusinessAudience
schemaClasses.label: BusinessAudience
schemaClasses.description: A set of characteristics belonging to businesses, e.g. who compose an item's target audience.

schemaClasses.name: BusinessEntityType
schemaClasses.label: BusinessEntityType
schemaClasses.description:
A business entity type is a conceptual entity representing the legal form, the size, the main line of business, the position in the value chain, or any combination thereof, of an organization or business person.

Commonly used values:

* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Business
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Enduser
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#PublicInstitution
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Reseller

schemaClasses.name: BusinessEvent
schemaClasses.label: BusinessEvent
schemaClasses.description: Event type: Business event.

schemaClasses.name: BusinessFunction
schemaClasses.label: BusinessFunction
schemaClasses.description:
The business function specifies the type of activity or access (i.e., the bundle of rights) offered by the organization or business person through the offer. Typical are sell, rental or lease, maintenance or repair, manufacture / produce, recycle / dispose, engineering / construction, or installation. Proprietary specifications of access rights are also instances of this class.

Commonly used values:

* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#ConstructionInstallation
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Dispose
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#LeaseOut
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Maintain
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#ProvideService
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Repair
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Sell
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Buy

schemaClasses.name: BuyAction
schemaClasses.label: BuyAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of giving money to a seller in exchange for goods or services rendered. An agent buys an object, product, or service from a seller for a price. Reciprocal of SellAction.

schemaClasses.name: CDCPMDRecord
schemaClasses.label: CDCPMDRecord
schemaClasses.description:
A CDCPMDRecord is a data structure representing a record in a CDC tabular data format
      used for hospital data reporting. See [documentation](/docs/cdc-covid.html) for details, and the linked CDC materials for authoritative
      definitions used as the source here.

schemaClasses.name: CableOrSatelliteService
schemaClasses.label: CableOrSatelliteService
schemaClasses.description: A service which provides access to media programming like TV or radio. Access may be via cable or satellite.

schemaClasses.name: CafeOrCoffeeShop
schemaClasses.label: CafeOrCoffeeShop
schemaClasses.description: A cafe or coffee shop.

schemaClasses.name: Campground
schemaClasses.label: Campground
schemaClasses.description:
A camping site, campsite, or [[Campground]] is a place used for overnight stay in the outdoors, typically containing individual [[CampingPitch]] locations. 


In British English a campsite is an area, usually divided into a number of pitches, where people can camp overnight using tents or camper vans or caravans; this British English use of the word is synonymous with the American English expression campground. In American English the term campsite generally means an area where an individual, family, group, or military unit can pitch a tent or park a camper; a campground may contain many campsites (source: Wikipedia, see [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Campsite](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Campsite)).



See also the dedicated [document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations](/docs/hotels.html).

schemaClasses.name: CampingPitch
schemaClasses.label: CampingPitch
schemaClasses.description:
A [[CampingPitch]] is an individual place for overnight stay in the outdoors, typically being part of a larger camping site, or [[Campground]].


In British English a campsite, or campground, is an area, usually divided into a number of pitches, where people can camp overnight using tents or camper vans or caravans; this British English use of the word is synonymous with the American English expression campground. In American English the term campsite generally means an area where an individual, family, group, or military unit can pitch a tent or park a camper; a campground may contain many campsites.
(Source: Wikipedia, see [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Campsite](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Campsite).)


See also the dedicated [document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations](/docs/hotels.html).

schemaClasses.name: Canal
schemaClasses.label: Canal
schemaClasses.description: A canal, like the Panama Canal.

schemaClasses.name: CancelAction
schemaClasses.label: CancelAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of asserting that a future event/action is no longer going to happen.

Related actions:

* [[ConfirmAction]]: The antonym of CancelAction.

schemaClasses.name: Car
schemaClasses.label: Car
schemaClasses.description: A car is a wheeled, self-powered motor vehicle used for transportation.

schemaClasses.name: CarUsageType
schemaClasses.label: CarUsageType
schemaClasses.description: A value indicating a special usage of a car, e.g. commercial rental, driving school, or as a taxi.

schemaClasses.name: Casino
schemaClasses.label: Casino
schemaClasses.description: A casino.

schemaClasses.name: CategoryCode
schemaClasses.label: CategoryCode
schemaClasses.description: A Category Code.

schemaClasses.name: CategoryCodeSet
schemaClasses.label: CategoryCodeSet
schemaClasses.description: A set of Category Code values.

schemaClasses.name: CatholicChurch
schemaClasses.label: CatholicChurch
schemaClasses.description: A Catholic church.

schemaClasses.name: Cemetery
schemaClasses.label: Cemetery
schemaClasses.description: A graveyard.

schemaClasses.name: Certification
schemaClasses.label: Certification
schemaClasses.description: A Certification is an official and authoritative statement about a subject, for example a product, service, person, or organization. A certification is typically issued by an indendent certification body, for example a professional organization or government. It formally attests certain characteristics about the subject, for example Organizations can be ISO certified, Food products can be certified Organic or Vegan, a Person can be a certified professional, a Place can be certified for food processing. There are certifications for many domains: regulatory, organizational, recycling, food, efficiency, educational, ecological, etc. A certification is a form of credential, as are accreditations and licenses. Mapped from the [gs1:CertificationDetails](https://www.gs1.org/voc/CertificationDetails) class in the GS1 Web Vocabulary.

schemaClasses.name: CertificationStatusEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: CertificationStatusEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Enumerates the different statuses of a Certification (Active and Inactive).

schemaClasses.name: Chapter
schemaClasses.label: Chapter
schemaClasses.description: One of the sections into which a book is divided. A chapter usually has a section number or a name.

schemaClasses.name: CheckAction
schemaClasses.label: CheckAction
schemaClasses.description: An agent inspects, determines, investigates, inquires, or examines an object's accuracy, quality, condition, or state.

schemaClasses.name: CheckInAction
schemaClasses.label: CheckInAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of an agent communicating (service provider, social media, etc) their arrival by registering/confirming for a previously reserved service (e.g. flight check-in) or at a place (e.g. hotel), possibly resulting in a result (boarding pass, etc).

Related actions:

* [[CheckOutAction]]: The antonym of CheckInAction.
* [[ArriveAction]]: Unlike ArriveAction, CheckInAction implies that the agent is informing/confirming the start of a previously reserved service.
* [[ConfirmAction]]: Unlike ConfirmAction, CheckInAction implies that the agent is informing/confirming the *start* of a previously reserved service rather than its validity/existence.

schemaClasses.name: CheckOutAction
schemaClasses.label: CheckOutAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of an agent communicating (service provider, social media, etc) their departure of a previously reserved service (e.g. flight check-in) or place (e.g. hotel).

Related actions:

* [[CheckInAction]]: The antonym of CheckOutAction.
* [[DepartAction]]: Unlike DepartAction, CheckOutAction implies that the agent is informing/confirming the end of a previously reserved service.
* [[CancelAction]]: Unlike CancelAction, CheckOutAction implies that the agent is informing/confirming the end of a previously reserved service.

schemaClasses.name: CheckoutPage
schemaClasses.label: CheckoutPage
schemaClasses.description: Web page type: Checkout page.

schemaClasses.name: ChemicalSubstance
schemaClasses.label: ChemicalSubstance
schemaClasses.description: A chemical substance is 'a portion of matter of constant composition, composed of molecular entities of the same type or of different types' (source: [ChEBI:59999](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/chebi/searchId.do?chebiId=59999)).

schemaClasses.name: ChildCare
schemaClasses.label: ChildCare
schemaClasses.description: A Childcare center.

schemaClasses.name: ChildrensEvent
schemaClasses.label: ChildrensEvent
schemaClasses.description: Event type: Children's event.

schemaClasses.name: ChooseAction
schemaClasses.label: ChooseAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of expressing a preference from a set of options or a large or unbounded set of choices/options.

schemaClasses.name: Church
schemaClasses.label: Church
schemaClasses.description: A church.

schemaClasses.name: City
schemaClasses.label: City
schemaClasses.description: A city or town.

schemaClasses.name: CityHall
schemaClasses.label: CityHall
schemaClasses.description: A city hall.

schemaClasses.name: CivicStructure
schemaClasses.label: CivicStructure
schemaClasses.description: A public structure, such as a town hall or concert hall.

schemaClasses.name: Claim
schemaClasses.label: Claim
schemaClasses.description:
A [[Claim]] in Schema.org represents a specific, factually-oriented claim that could be the [[itemReviewed]] in a [[ClaimReview]]. The content of a claim can be summarized with the [[text]] property. Variations on well known claims can have their common identity indicated via [[sameAs]] links, and summarized with a [[name]]. Ideally, a [[Claim]] description includes enough contextual information to minimize the risk of ambiguity or inclarity. In practice, many claims are better understood in the context in which they appear or the interpretations provided by claim reviews.

  Beyond [[ClaimReview]], the Claim type can be associated with related creative works - for example a [[ScholarlyArticle]] or [[Question]] might be [[about]] some [[Claim]].

  At this time, Schema.org does not define any types of relationship between claims. This is a natural area for future exploration.

schemaClasses.name: ClaimReview
schemaClasses.label: ClaimReview
schemaClasses.description: A fact-checking review of claims made (or reported) in some creative work (referenced via itemReviewed).

schemaClasses.name: Class
schemaClasses.label: Class
schemaClasses.description: A class, also often called a 'Type'; equivalent to rdfs:Class.

schemaClasses.name: Clip
schemaClasses.label: Clip
schemaClasses.description: A short TV or radio program or a segment/part of a program.

schemaClasses.name: ClothingStore
schemaClasses.label: ClothingStore
schemaClasses.description: A clothing store.

schemaClasses.name: Code
schemaClasses.label: Code
schemaClasses.description: Computer programming source code. Example: Full (compile ready) solutions, code snippet samples, scripts, templates.

schemaClasses.name: Collection
schemaClasses.label: Collection
schemaClasses.description: A collection of items, e.g. creative works or products.

schemaClasses.name: CollectionPage
schemaClasses.label: CollectionPage
schemaClasses.description: Web page type: Collection page.

schemaClasses.name: CollegeOrUniversity
schemaClasses.label: CollegeOrUniversity
schemaClasses.description: A college, university, or other third-level educational institution.

schemaClasses.name: ComedyClub
schemaClasses.label: ComedyClub
schemaClasses.description: A comedy club.

schemaClasses.name: ComedyEvent
schemaClasses.label: ComedyEvent
schemaClasses.description: Event type: Comedy event.

schemaClasses.name: ComicCoverArt
schemaClasses.label: ComicCoverArt
schemaClasses.description: The artwork on the cover of a comic.

schemaClasses.name: ComicIssue
schemaClasses.label: ComicIssue
schemaClasses.description:
Individual comic issues are serially published as
    	part of a larger series. For the sake of consistency, even one-shot issues
    	belong to a series comprised of a single issue. All comic issues can be
    	uniquely identified by: the combination of the name and volume number of the
    	series to which the issue belongs; the issue number; and the variant
    	description of the issue (if any).

schemaClasses.name: ComicSeries
schemaClasses.label: ComicSeries
schemaClasses.description:
A sequential publication of comic stories under a
    	unifying title, for example "The Amazing Spider-Man" or "Groo the
    	Wanderer".

schemaClasses.name: ComicStory
schemaClasses.label: ComicStory
schemaClasses.description:
The term "story" is any indivisible, re-printable
    	unit of a comic, including the interior stories, covers, and backmatter. Most
    	comics have at least two stories: a cover (ComicCoverArt) and an interior story.

schemaClasses.name: Comment
schemaClasses.label: Comment
schemaClasses.description: A comment on an item - for example, a comment on a blog post. The comment's content is expressed via the [[text]] property, and its topic via [[about]], properties shared with all CreativeWorks.

schemaClasses.name: CommentAction
schemaClasses.label: CommentAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of generating a comment about a subject.

schemaClasses.name: CommunicateAction
schemaClasses.label: CommunicateAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of conveying information to another person via a communication medium (instrument) such as speech, email, or telephone conversation.

schemaClasses.name: CompleteDataFeed
schemaClasses.label: CompleteDataFeed
schemaClasses.description:
A [[CompleteDataFeed]] is a [[DataFeed]] whose standard representation includes content for every item currently in the feed.

This is the equivalent of Atom's element as defined in Feed Paging and Archiving [RFC 5005](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5005), for example (and as defined for Atom), when using data from a feed that represents a collection of items that varies over time (e.g. "Top Twenty Records") there is no need to have newer entries mixed in alongside older, obsolete entries. By marking this feed as a CompleteDataFeed, old entries can be safely discarded when the feed is refreshed, since we can assume the feed has provided descriptions for all current items.

schemaClasses.name: CompoundPriceSpecification
schemaClasses.label: CompoundPriceSpecification
schemaClasses.description: A compound price specification is one that bundles multiple prices that all apply in combination for different dimensions of consumption. Use the name property of the attached unit price specification for indicating the dimension of a price component (e.g. "electricity" or "final cleaning").

schemaClasses.name: ComputerLanguage
schemaClasses.label: ComputerLanguage
schemaClasses.description: This type covers computer programming languages such as Scheme and Lisp, as well as other language-like computer representations. Natural languages are best represented with the [[Language]] type.

schemaClasses.name: ComputerStore
schemaClasses.label: ComputerStore
schemaClasses.description: A computer store.

schemaClasses.name: ConferenceEvent
schemaClasses.label: ConferenceEvent
schemaClasses.description: Event type: Conference event.

schemaClasses.name: ConfirmAction
schemaClasses.label: ConfirmAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of notifying someone that a future event/action is going to happen as expected.

Related actions:

* [[CancelAction]]: The antonym of ConfirmAction.

schemaClasses.name: Consortium
schemaClasses.label: Consortium
schemaClasses.description: A Consortium is a membership [[Organization]] whose members are typically Organizations.

schemaClasses.name: ConstraintNode
schemaClasses.label: ConstraintNode
schemaClasses.description:
The ConstraintNode type is provided to support usecases in which a node in a structured data graph is described with properties which appear to describe a single entity, but are being used in a situation where they serve a more abstract purpose. A [[ConstraintNode]] can be described using [[constraintProperty]] and [[numConstraints]]. These constraint properties can serve a
    variety of purposes, and their values may sometimes be understood to indicate sets of possible values rather than single, exact and specific values.

schemaClasses.name: ConsumeAction
schemaClasses.label: ConsumeAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of ingesting information/resources/food.

schemaClasses.name: ContactPage
schemaClasses.label: ContactPage
schemaClasses.description: Web page type: Contact page.

schemaClasses.name: ContactPoint
schemaClasses.label: ContactPoint
schemaClasses.description: A contact point—for example, a Customer Complaints department.

schemaClasses.name: ContactPointOption
schemaClasses.label: ContactPointOption
schemaClasses.description: Enumerated options related to a ContactPoint.

schemaClasses.name: Continent
schemaClasses.label: Continent
schemaClasses.description: One of the continents (for example, Europe or Africa).

schemaClasses.name: ControlAction
schemaClasses.label: ControlAction
schemaClasses.description: An agent controls a device or application.

schemaClasses.name: ConvenienceStore
schemaClasses.label: ConvenienceStore
schemaClasses.description: A convenience store.

schemaClasses.name: Conversation
schemaClasses.label: Conversation
schemaClasses.description: One or more messages between organizations or people on a particular topic. Individual messages can be linked to the conversation with isPartOf or hasPart properties.

schemaClasses.name: CookAction
schemaClasses.label: CookAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of producing/preparing food.

schemaClasses.name: Cooperative
schemaClasses.label: Cooperative
schemaClasses.description: An organization that is a joint project of multiple organizations or persons.

schemaClasses.name: Corporation
schemaClasses.label: Corporation
schemaClasses.description: Organization: A business corporation.

schemaClasses.name: CorrectionComment
schemaClasses.label: CorrectionComment
schemaClasses.description: A [[comment]] that corrects [[CreativeWork]].

schemaClasses.name: Country
schemaClasses.label: Country
schemaClasses.description: A country.

schemaClasses.name: Course
schemaClasses.label: Course
schemaClasses.description: A description of an educational course which may be offered as distinct instances which take place at different times or take place at different locations, or be offered through different media or modes of study. An educational course is a sequence of one or more educational events and/or creative works which aims to build knowledge, competence or ability of learners.

schemaClasses.name: CourseInstance
schemaClasses.label: CourseInstance
schemaClasses.description: An instance of a [[Course]] which is distinct from other instances because it is offered at a different time or location or through different media or modes of study or to a specific section of students.

schemaClasses.name: Courthouse
schemaClasses.label: Courthouse
schemaClasses.description: A courthouse.

schemaClasses.name: CoverArt
schemaClasses.label: CoverArt
schemaClasses.description: The artwork on the outer surface of a CreativeWork.

schemaClasses.name: CovidTestingFacility
schemaClasses.label: CovidTestingFacility
schemaClasses.description:
A CovidTestingFacility is a [[MedicalClinic]] where testing for the COVID-19 Coronavirus
      disease is available. If the facility is being made available from an established [[Pharmacy]], [[Hotel]], or other
      non-medical organization, multiple types can be listed. This makes it easier to re-use existing schema.org information
      about that place, e.g. contact info, address, opening hours. Note that in an emergency, such information may not always be reliable.

schemaClasses.name: CreateAction
schemaClasses.label: CreateAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of deliberately creating/producing/generating/building a result out of the agent.

schemaClasses.name: CreativeWork
schemaClasses.label: CreativeWork
schemaClasses.description: The most generic kind of creative work, including books, movies, photographs, software programs, etc.

schemaClasses.name: CreativeWorkSeason
schemaClasses.label: CreativeWorkSeason
schemaClasses.description: A media season, e.g. TV, radio, video game etc.

schemaClasses.name: CreativeWorkSeries
schemaClasses.label: CreativeWorkSeries
schemaClasses.description:
A CreativeWorkSeries in schema.org is a group of related items, typically but not necessarily of the same kind. CreativeWorkSeries are usually organized into some order, often chronological. Unlike [[ItemList]] which is a general purpose data structure for lists of things, the emphasis with CreativeWorkSeries is on published materials (written e.g. books and periodicals, or media such as TV, radio and games).

Specific subtypes are available for describing [[TVSeries]], [[RadioSeries]], [[MovieSeries]], [[BookSeries]], [[Periodical]] and [[VideoGameSeries]]. In each case, the [[hasPart]] / [[isPartOf]] properties can be used to relate the CreativeWorkSeries to its parts. The general CreativeWorkSeries type serves largely just to organize these more specific and practical subtypes.

It is common for properties applicable to an item from the series to be usefully applied to the containing group. Schema.org attempts to anticipate some of these cases, but publishers should be free to apply properties of the series parts to the series as a whole wherever they seem appropriate.

schemaClasses.name: CreditCard
schemaClasses.label: CreditCard
schemaClasses.description:
A card payment method of a particular brand or name.  Used to mark up a particular payment method and/or the financial product/service that supplies the card account.

Commonly used values:

* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#AmericanExpress
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#DinersClub
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Discover
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#JCB
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#MasterCard
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#VISA

schemaClasses.name: Crematorium
schemaClasses.label: Crematorium
schemaClasses.description: A crematorium.

schemaClasses.name: CriticReview
schemaClasses.label: CriticReview
schemaClasses.description: A [[CriticReview]] is a more specialized form of Review written or published by a source that is recognized for its reviewing activities. These can include online columns, travel and food guides, TV and radio shows, blogs and other independent Web sites. [[CriticReview]]s are typically more in-depth and professionally written. For simpler, casually written user/visitor/viewer/customer reviews, it is more appropriate to use the [[UserReview]] type. Review aggregator sites such as Metacritic already separate out the site's user reviews from selected critic reviews that originate from third-party sources.

schemaClasses.name: CssSelectorType
schemaClasses.label: CssSelectorType
schemaClasses.description: Text representing a CSS selector.

schemaClasses.name: CurrencyConversionService
schemaClasses.label: CurrencyConversionService
schemaClasses.description: A service to convert funds from one currency to another currency.

schemaClasses.name: DDxElement
schemaClasses.label: DDxElement
schemaClasses.description: An alternative, closely-related condition typically considered later in the differential diagnosis process along with the signs that are used to distinguish it.

schemaClasses.name: DanceEvent
schemaClasses.label: DanceEvent
schemaClasses.description: Event type: A social dance.

schemaClasses.name: DanceGroup
schemaClasses.label: DanceGroup
schemaClasses.description: A dance group—for example, the Alvin Ailey Dance Theater or Riverdance.

schemaClasses.name: DataCatalog
schemaClasses.label: DataCatalog
schemaClasses.description: A collection of datasets.

schemaClasses.name: DataDownload
schemaClasses.label: DataDownload
schemaClasses.description: All or part of a [[Dataset]] in downloadable form.

schemaClasses.name: DataFeed
schemaClasses.label: DataFeed
schemaClasses.description: A single feed providing structured information about one or more entities or topics.

schemaClasses.name: DataFeedItem
schemaClasses.label: DataFeedItem
schemaClasses.description: A single item within a larger data feed.

schemaClasses.name: DataType
schemaClasses.label: DataType
schemaClasses.description: The basic data types such as Integers, Strings, etc.

schemaClasses.name: Dataset
schemaClasses.label: Dataset
schemaClasses.description: A body of structured information describing some topic(s) of interest.

schemaClasses.name: Date
schemaClasses.label: Date
schemaClasses.description: A date value in [ISO 8601 date format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

schemaClasses.name: DateTime
schemaClasses.label: DateTime
schemaClasses.description: A combination of date and time of day in the form [-]CCYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss[Z|(+|-)hh:mm] (see Chapter 5.4 of ISO 8601).

schemaClasses.name: DatedMoneySpecification
schemaClasses.label: DatedMoneySpecification
schemaClasses.description: A DatedMoneySpecification represents monetary values with optional start and end dates. For example, this could represent an employee's salary over a specific period of time. __Note:__ This type has been superseded by [[MonetaryAmount]], use of that type is recommended.

schemaClasses.name: DayOfWeek
schemaClasses.label: DayOfWeek
schemaClasses.description:
The day of the week, e.g. used to specify to which day the opening hours of an OpeningHoursSpecification refer.

Originally, URLs from [GoodRelations](http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1) were used (for [[Monday]], [[Tuesday]], [[Wednesday]], [[Thursday]], [[Friday]], [[Saturday]], [[Sunday]] plus a special entry for [[PublicHolidays]]); these have now been integrated directly into schema.org.

schemaClasses.name: DaySpa
schemaClasses.label: DaySpa
schemaClasses.description: A day spa.

schemaClasses.name: DeactivateAction
schemaClasses.label: DeactivateAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of stopping or deactivating a device or application (e.g. stopping a timer or turning off a flashlight).

schemaClasses.name: DefenceEstablishment
schemaClasses.label: DefenceEstablishment
schemaClasses.description: A defence establishment, such as an army or navy base.

schemaClasses.name: DefinedRegion
schemaClasses.label: DefinedRegion
schemaClasses.description:
A DefinedRegion is a geographic area defined by potentially arbitrary (rather than political, administrative or natural geographical) criteria. Properties are provided for defining a region by reference to sets of postal codes.

Examples: a delivery destination when shopping. Region where regional pricing is configured.

Requirement 1:
Country: US
States: "NY", "CA"

Requirement 2:
Country: US
PostalCode Set: { [94000-94585], [97000, 97999], [13000, 13599]}
{ [12345, 12345], [78945, 78945], }
Region = state, canton, prefecture, autonomous community...

schemaClasses.name: DefinedTerm
schemaClasses.label: DefinedTerm
schemaClasses.description: A word, name, acronym, phrase, etc. with a formal definition. Often used in the context of category or subject classification, glossaries or dictionaries, product or creative work types, etc. Use the name property for the term being defined, use termCode if the term has an alpha-numeric code allocated, use description to provide the definition of the term. Use the about property to specify what the term is about.

schemaClasses.name: DefinedTermSet
schemaClasses.label: DefinedTermSet
schemaClasses.description: A set of defined terms, for example a set of categories or a classification scheme, a glossary, dictionary or enumeration. Use the about property to specify what the term set is about.

schemaClasses.name: DeleteAction
schemaClasses.label: DeleteAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of editing a recipient by removing one of its objects.

schemaClasses.name: DeliveryChargeSpecification
schemaClasses.label: DeliveryChargeSpecification
schemaClasses.description: The price for the delivery of an offer using a particular delivery method.

schemaClasses.name: DeliveryEvent
schemaClasses.label: DeliveryEvent
schemaClasses.description: An event involving the delivery of an item.

schemaClasses.name: DeliveryMethod
schemaClasses.label: DeliveryMethod
schemaClasses.description:
A delivery method is a standardized procedure for transferring the product or service to the destination of fulfillment chosen by the customer. Delivery methods are characterized by the means of transportation used, and by the organization or group that is the contracting party for the sending organization or person.

Commonly used values:

* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#DeliveryModeDirectDownload
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#DeliveryModeFreight
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#DeliveryModeMail
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#DeliveryModeOwnFleet
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#DeliveryModePickUp
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#DHL
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#FederalExpress
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#UPS

schemaClasses.name: Demand
schemaClasses.label: Demand
schemaClasses.description: A demand entity represents the public, not necessarily binding, not necessarily exclusive, announcement by an organization or person to seek a certain type of goods or services. For describing demand using this type, the very same properties used for Offer apply.

schemaClasses.name: Dentist
schemaClasses.label: Dentist
schemaClasses.description: A dentist.

schemaClasses.name: DepartAction
schemaClasses.label: DepartAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of  departing from a place. An agent departs from a fromLocation for a destination, optionally with participants.

schemaClasses.name: DepartmentStore
schemaClasses.label: DepartmentStore
schemaClasses.description: A department store.

schemaClasses.name: DepositAccount
schemaClasses.label: DepositAccount
schemaClasses.description: A type of Bank Account with a main purpose of depositing funds to gain interest or other benefits.

schemaClasses.name: DiagnosticLab
schemaClasses.label: DiagnosticLab
schemaClasses.description: A medical laboratory that offers on-site or off-site diagnostic services.

schemaClasses.name: DiagnosticProcedure
schemaClasses.label: DiagnosticProcedure
schemaClasses.description: A medical procedure intended primarily for diagnostic, as opposed to therapeutic, purposes.

schemaClasses.name: Diet
schemaClasses.label: Diet
schemaClasses.description: A strategy of regulating the intake of food to achieve or maintain a specific health-related goal.

schemaClasses.name: DietarySupplement
schemaClasses.label: DietarySupplement
schemaClasses.description: A product taken by mouth that contains a dietary ingredient intended to supplement the diet. Dietary ingredients may include vitamins, minerals, herbs or other botanicals, amino acids, and substances such as enzymes, organ tissues, glandulars and metabolites.

schemaClasses.name: DigitalDocument
schemaClasses.label: DigitalDocument
schemaClasses.description: An electronic file or document.

schemaClasses.name: DigitalDocumentPermission
schemaClasses.label: DigitalDocumentPermission
schemaClasses.description: A permission for a particular person or group to access a particular file.

schemaClasses.name: DigitalDocumentPermissionType
schemaClasses.label: DigitalDocumentPermissionType
schemaClasses.description: A type of permission which can be granted for accessing a digital document.

schemaClasses.name: DigitalPlatformEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: DigitalPlatformEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Enumerates some common technology platforms, for use with properties such as [[actionPlatform]]. It is not supposed to be comprehensive - when a suitable code is not enumerated here, textual or URL values can be used instead. These codes are at a fairly high level and do not deal with versioning and other nuance. Additional codes can be suggested [in github](https://github.com/schemaorg/schemaorg/issues/3057).

schemaClasses.name: DisagreeAction
schemaClasses.label: DisagreeAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of expressing a difference of opinion with the object. An agent disagrees to/about an object (a proposition, topic or theme) with participants.

schemaClasses.name: DiscoverAction
schemaClasses.label: DiscoverAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of discovering/finding an object.

schemaClasses.name: DiscussionForumPosting
schemaClasses.label: DiscussionForumPosting
schemaClasses.description: A posting to a discussion forum.

schemaClasses.name: DislikeAction
schemaClasses.label: DislikeAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of expressing a negative sentiment about the object. An agent dislikes an object (a proposition, topic or theme) with participants.

schemaClasses.name: Distance
schemaClasses.label: Distance
schemaClasses.description: Properties that take Distances as values are of the form '<Number> <Length unit of measure>'. E.g., '7 ft'.

schemaClasses.name: Distillery
schemaClasses.label: Distillery
schemaClasses.description: A distillery.

schemaClasses.name: DonateAction
schemaClasses.label: DonateAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of providing goods, services, or money without compensation, often for philanthropic reasons.

schemaClasses.name: DoseSchedule
schemaClasses.label: DoseSchedule
schemaClasses.description: A specific dosing schedule for a drug or supplement.

schemaClasses.name: DownloadAction
schemaClasses.label: DownloadAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of downloading an object.

schemaClasses.name: DrawAction
schemaClasses.label: DrawAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of producing a visual/graphical representation of an object, typically with a pen/pencil and paper as instruments.

schemaClasses.name: Drawing
schemaClasses.label: Drawing
schemaClasses.description: A picture or diagram made with a pencil, pen, or crayon rather than paint.

schemaClasses.name: DrinkAction
schemaClasses.label: DrinkAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of swallowing liquids.

schemaClasses.name: DriveWheelConfigurationValue
schemaClasses.label: DriveWheelConfigurationValue
schemaClasses.description: A value indicating which roadwheels will receive torque.

schemaClasses.name: Drug
schemaClasses.label: Drug
schemaClasses.description: A chemical or biologic substance, used as a medical therapy, that has a physiological effect on an organism. Here the term drug is used interchangeably with the term medicine although clinical knowledge makes a clear difference between them.

schemaClasses.name: DrugClass
schemaClasses.label: DrugClass
schemaClasses.description: A class of medical drugs, e.g., statins. Classes can represent general pharmacological class, common mechanisms of action, common physiological effects, etc.

schemaClasses.name: DrugCost
schemaClasses.label: DrugCost
schemaClasses.description: The cost per unit of a medical drug. Note that this type is not meant to represent the price in an offer of a drug for sale; see the Offer type for that. This type will typically be used to tag wholesale or average retail cost of a drug, or maximum reimbursable cost. Costs of medical drugs vary widely depending on how and where they are paid for, so while this type captures some of the variables, costs should be used with caution by consumers of this schema's markup.

schemaClasses.name: DrugCostCategory
schemaClasses.label: DrugCostCategory
schemaClasses.description: Enumerated categories of medical drug costs.

schemaClasses.name: DrugLegalStatus
schemaClasses.label: DrugLegalStatus
schemaClasses.description: The legal availability status of a medical drug.

schemaClasses.name: DrugPregnancyCategory
schemaClasses.label: DrugPregnancyCategory
schemaClasses.description: Categories that represent an assessment of the risk of fetal injury due to a drug or pharmaceutical used as directed by the mother during pregnancy.

schemaClasses.name: DrugPrescriptionStatus
schemaClasses.label: DrugPrescriptionStatus
schemaClasses.description: Indicates whether this drug is available by prescription or over-the-counter.

schemaClasses.name: DrugStrength
schemaClasses.label: DrugStrength
schemaClasses.description: A specific strength in which a medical drug is available in a specific country.

schemaClasses.name: DryCleaningOrLaundry
schemaClasses.label: DryCleaningOrLaundry
schemaClasses.description: A dry-cleaning business.

schemaClasses.name: Duration
schemaClasses.label: Duration
schemaClasses.description: Quantity: Duration (use [ISO 8601 duration format](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601)).

schemaClasses.name: EUEnergyEfficiencyEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: EUEnergyEfficiencyEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Enumerates the EU energy efficiency classes A-G as well as A+, A++, and A+++ as defined in EU directive 2017/1369.

schemaClasses.name: EatAction
schemaClasses.label: EatAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of swallowing solid objects.

schemaClasses.name: EducationEvent
schemaClasses.label: EducationEvent
schemaClasses.description: Event type: Education event.

schemaClasses.name: EducationalAudience
schemaClasses.label: EducationalAudience
schemaClasses.description: An EducationalAudience.

schemaClasses.name: EducationalOccupationalCredential
schemaClasses.label: EducationalOccupationalCredential
schemaClasses.description: An educational or occupational credential. A diploma, academic degree, certification, qualification, badge, etc., that may be awarded to a person or other entity that meets the requirements defined by the credentialer.

schemaClasses.name: EducationalOccupationalProgram
schemaClasses.label: EducationalOccupationalProgram
schemaClasses.description: A program offered by an institution which determines the learning progress to achieve an outcome, usually a credential like a degree or certificate. This would define a discrete set of opportunities (e.g., job, courses) that together constitute a program with a clear start, end, set of requirements, and transition to a new occupational opportunity (e.g., a job), or sometimes a higher educational opportunity (e.g., an advanced degree).

schemaClasses.name: EducationalOrganization
schemaClasses.label: EducationalOrganization
schemaClasses.description: An educational organization.

schemaClasses.name: Electrician
schemaClasses.label: Electrician
schemaClasses.description: An electrician.

schemaClasses.name: ElectronicsStore
schemaClasses.label: ElectronicsStore
schemaClasses.description: An electronics store.

schemaClasses.name: ElementarySchool
schemaClasses.label: ElementarySchool
schemaClasses.description: An elementary school.

schemaClasses.name: EmailMessage
schemaClasses.label: EmailMessage
schemaClasses.description: An email message.

schemaClasses.name: Embassy
schemaClasses.label: Embassy
schemaClasses.description: An embassy.

schemaClasses.name: EmergencyService
schemaClasses.label: EmergencyService
schemaClasses.description: An emergency service, such as a fire station or ER.

schemaClasses.name: EmployeeRole
schemaClasses.label: EmployeeRole
schemaClasses.description: A subclass of OrganizationRole used to describe employee relationships.

schemaClasses.name: EmployerAggregateRating
schemaClasses.label: EmployerAggregateRating
schemaClasses.description: An aggregate rating of an Organization related to its role as an employer.

schemaClasses.name: EmployerReview
schemaClasses.label: EmployerReview
schemaClasses.description: An [[EmployerReview]] is a review of an [[Organization]] regarding its role as an employer, written by a current or former employee of that organization.

schemaClasses.name: EmploymentAgency
schemaClasses.label: EmploymentAgency
schemaClasses.description: An employment agency.

schemaClasses.name: EndorseAction
schemaClasses.label: EndorseAction
schemaClasses.description: An agent approves/certifies/likes/supports/sanctions an object.

schemaClasses.name: EndorsementRating
schemaClasses.label: EndorsementRating
schemaClasses.description:
An EndorsementRating is a rating that expresses some level of endorsement, for example inclusion in a "critic's pick" blog, a
"Like" or "+1" on a social network. It can be considered the [[result]] of an [[EndorseAction]] in which the [[object]] of the action is rated positively by
some [[agent]]. As is common elsewhere in schema.org, it is sometimes more useful to describe the results of such an action without explicitly describing the [[Action]].

An [[EndorsementRating]] may be part of a numeric scale or organized system, but this is not required: having an explicit type for indicating a positive,
endorsement rating is particularly useful in the absence of numeric scales as it helps consumers understand that the rating is broadly positive.

schemaClasses.name: Energy
schemaClasses.label: Energy
schemaClasses.description: Properties that take Energy as values are of the form '<Number> <Energy unit of measure>'.

schemaClasses.name: EnergyConsumptionDetails
schemaClasses.label: EnergyConsumptionDetails
schemaClasses.description: EnergyConsumptionDetails represents information related to the energy efficiency of a product that consumes energy. The information that can be provided is based on international regulations such as for example [EU directive 2017/1369](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2017/1369/oj) for energy labeling and the [Energy labeling rule](https://www.ftc.gov/enforcement/rules/rulemaking-regulatory-reform-proceedings/energy-water-use-labeling-consumer) under the Energy Policy and Conservation Act (EPCA) in the US.

schemaClasses.name: EnergyEfficiencyEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: EnergyEfficiencyEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Enumerates energy efficiency levels (also known as "classes" or "ratings") and certifications that are part of several international energy efficiency standards.

schemaClasses.name: EnergyStarEnergyEfficiencyEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: EnergyStarEnergyEfficiencyEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Used to indicate whether a product is EnergyStar certified.

schemaClasses.name: EngineSpecification
schemaClasses.label: EngineSpecification
schemaClasses.description: Information about the engine of the vehicle. A vehicle can have multiple engines represented by multiple engine specification entities.

schemaClasses.name: EntertainmentBusiness
schemaClasses.label: EntertainmentBusiness
schemaClasses.description: A business providing entertainment.

schemaClasses.name: EntryPoint
schemaClasses.label: EntryPoint
schemaClasses.description: An entry point, within some Web-based protocol.

schemaClasses.name: Enumeration
schemaClasses.label: Enumeration
schemaClasses.description: Lists or enumerations—for example, a list of cuisines or music genres, etc.

schemaClasses.name: Episode
schemaClasses.label: Episode
schemaClasses.description: A media episode (e.g. TV, radio, video game) which can be part of a series or season.

schemaClasses.name: Error
schemaClasses.label: Error
schemaClasses.description: Representation of an Error.

schemaClasses.name: Event
schemaClasses.label: Event
schemaClasses.description: An event happening at a certain time and location, such as a concert, lecture, or festival. Ticketing information may be added via the [[offers]] property. Repeated events may be structured as separate Event objects.

schemaClasses.name: EventAttendanceModeEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: EventAttendanceModeEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: An EventAttendanceModeEnumeration value is one of potentially several modes of organising an event, relating to whether it is online or offline.

schemaClasses.name: EventReservation
schemaClasses.label: EventReservation
schemaClasses.description: A reservation for an event like a concert, sporting event, or lecture.\n\nNote: This type is for information about actual reservations, e.g. in confirmation emails or HTML pages with individual confirmations of reservations. For offers of tickets, use [[Offer]].

schemaClasses.name: EventSeries
schemaClasses.label: EventSeries
schemaClasses.description:
A series of [[Event]]s. Included events can relate with the series using the [[superEvent]] property.

An EventSeries is a collection of events that share some unifying characteristic. For example, "The Olympic Games" is a series, which
is repeated regularly. The "2012 London Olympics" can be presented both as an [[Event]] in the series "Olympic Games", and as an
[[EventSeries]] that included a number of sporting competitions as Events.

The nature of the association between the events in an [[EventSeries]] can vary, but typical examples could
include a thematic event series (e.g. topical meetups or classes), or a series of regular events that share a location, attendee group and/or organizers.

EventSeries has been defined as a kind of Event to make it easy for publishers to use it in an Event context without
worrying about which kinds of series are really event-like enough to call an Event. In general an EventSeries
may seem more Event-like when the period of time is compact and when aspects such as location are fixed, but
it may also sometimes prove useful to describe a longer-term series as an Event.

schemaClasses.name: EventStatusType
schemaClasses.label: EventStatusType
schemaClasses.description: EventStatusType is an enumeration type whose instances represent several states that an Event may be in.

schemaClasses.name: EventVenue
schemaClasses.label: EventVenue
schemaClasses.description: An event venue.

schemaClasses.name: ExchangeRateSpecification
schemaClasses.label: ExchangeRateSpecification
schemaClasses.description: A structured value representing exchange rate.

schemaClasses.name: ExerciseAction
schemaClasses.label: ExerciseAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of participating in exertive activity for the purposes of improving health and fitness.

schemaClasses.name: ExerciseGym
schemaClasses.label: ExerciseGym
schemaClasses.description: A gym.

schemaClasses.name: ExercisePlan
schemaClasses.label: ExercisePlan
schemaClasses.description: Fitness-related activity designed for a specific health-related purpose, including defined exercise routines as well as activity prescribed by a clinician.

schemaClasses.name: ExhibitionEvent
schemaClasses.label: ExhibitionEvent
schemaClasses.description: Event type: Exhibition event, e.g. at a museum, library, archive, tradeshow, ...

schemaClasses.name: FAQPage
schemaClasses.label: FAQPage
schemaClasses.description: A [[FAQPage]] is a [[WebPage]] presenting one or more "[Frequently asked questions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FAQ)" (see also [[QAPage]]).

schemaClasses.name: FMRadioChannel
schemaClasses.label: FMRadioChannel
schemaClasses.description: A radio channel that uses FM.

schemaClasses.name: FastFoodRestaurant
schemaClasses.label: FastFoodRestaurant
schemaClasses.description: A fast-food restaurant.

schemaClasses.name: Festival
schemaClasses.label: Festival
schemaClasses.description: Event type: Festival.

schemaClasses.name: FilmAction
schemaClasses.label: FilmAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of capturing sound and moving images on film, video, or digitally.

schemaClasses.name: FinancialIncentive
schemaClasses.label: FinancialIncentive
schemaClasses.description:
Represents financial incentives for goods/services offered by an organization (or individual).

Typically contains the [[name]] of the incentive, the [[incentivizedItem]], the [[incentiveAmount]], the [[incentiveStatus]], [[incentiveType]], the [[provider]] of the incentive, and [[eligibleWithSupplier]].

Optionally contains criteria on whether the incentive is limited based on [[purchaseType]], [[purchasePriceLimit]], [[incomeLimit]], and the [[qualifiedExpense]].

schemaClasses.name: FinancialProduct
schemaClasses.label: FinancialProduct
schemaClasses.description: A product provided to consumers and businesses by financial institutions such as banks, insurance companies, brokerage firms, consumer finance companies, and investment companies which comprise the financial services industry.

schemaClasses.name: FinancialService
schemaClasses.label: FinancialService
schemaClasses.description: Financial services business.

schemaClasses.name: FindAction
schemaClasses.label: FindAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of finding an object.

Related actions:

* [[SearchAction]]: FindAction is generally lead by a SearchAction, but not necessarily.

schemaClasses.name: FireStation
schemaClasses.label: FireStation
schemaClasses.description: A fire station. With firemen.

schemaClasses.name: Flight
schemaClasses.label: Flight
schemaClasses.description: An airline flight.

schemaClasses.name: FlightReservation
schemaClasses.label: FlightReservation
schemaClasses.description: A reservation for air travel.\n\nNote: This type is for information about actual reservations, e.g. in confirmation emails or HTML pages with individual confirmations of reservations. For offers of tickets, use [[Offer]].

schemaClasses.name: Float
schemaClasses.label: Float
schemaClasses.description: Data type: Floating number.

schemaClasses.name: FloorPlan
schemaClasses.label: FloorPlan
schemaClasses.description: A FloorPlan is an explicit representation of a collection of similar accommodations, allowing the provision of common information (room counts, sizes, layout diagrams) and offers for rental or sale. In typical use, some [[ApartmentComplex]] has an [[accommodationFloorPlan]] which is a [[FloorPlan]].  A FloorPlan is always in the context of a particular place, either a larger [[ApartmentComplex]] or a single [[Apartment]]. The visual/spatial aspects of a floor plan (i.e. room layout, [see wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Floor_plan)) can be indicated using [[image]].

schemaClasses.name: Florist
schemaClasses.label: Florist
schemaClasses.description: A florist.

schemaClasses.name: FollowAction
schemaClasses.label: FollowAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of forming a personal connection with someone/something (object) unidirectionally/asymmetrically to get updates polled from.

Related actions:

* [[BefriendAction]]: Unlike BefriendAction, FollowAction implies that the connection is *not* necessarily reciprocal.
* [[SubscribeAction]]: Unlike SubscribeAction, FollowAction implies that the follower acts as an active agent constantly/actively polling for updates.
* [[RegisterAction]]: Unlike RegisterAction, FollowAction implies that the agent is interested in continuing receiving updates from the object.
* [[JoinAction]]: Unlike JoinAction, FollowAction implies that the agent is interested in getting updates from the object.
* [[TrackAction]]: Unlike TrackAction, FollowAction refers to the polling of updates of all aspects of animate objects rather than the location of inanimate objects (e.g. you track a package, but you don't follow it).

schemaClasses.name: FoodEstablishment
schemaClasses.label: FoodEstablishment
schemaClasses.description: A food-related business.

schemaClasses.name: FoodEstablishmentReservation
schemaClasses.label: FoodEstablishmentReservation
schemaClasses.description: A reservation to dine at a food-related business.\n\nNote: This type is for information about actual reservations, e.g. in confirmation emails or HTML pages with individual confirmations of reservations.

schemaClasses.name: FoodEvent
schemaClasses.label: FoodEvent
schemaClasses.description: Event type: Food event.

schemaClasses.name: FoodService
schemaClasses.label: FoodService
schemaClasses.description: A food service, like breakfast, lunch, or dinner.

schemaClasses.name: FulfillmentTypeEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: FulfillmentTypeEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: A type of product fulfillment.

schemaClasses.name: FundingAgency
schemaClasses.label: FundingAgency
schemaClasses.description:
A FundingAgency is an organization that implements one or more [[FundingScheme]]s and manages
    the granting process (via [[Grant]]s, typically [[MonetaryGrant]]s).
    A funding agency is not always required for grant funding, e.g. philanthropic giving, corporate sponsorship etc.
    
Examples of funding agencies include ERC, REA, NIH, Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation, ...

schemaClasses.name: FundingScheme
schemaClasses.label: FundingScheme
schemaClasses.description:
A FundingScheme combines organizational, project and policy aspects of grant-based funding
    that sets guidelines, principles and mechanisms to support other kinds of projects and activities.
    Funding is typically organized via [[Grant]] funding. Examples of funding schemes: Swiss Priority Programmes (SPPs); EU Framework 7 (FP7); Horizon 2020; the NIH-R01 Grant Program; Wellcome institutional strategic support fund. For large scale public sector funding, the management and administration of grant awards is often handled by other, dedicated, organizations - [[FundingAgency]]s such as ERC, REA, ...

schemaClasses.name: FurnitureStore
schemaClasses.label: FurnitureStore
schemaClasses.description: A furniture store.

schemaClasses.name: Game
schemaClasses.label: Game
schemaClasses.description: The Game type represents things which are games. These are typically rule-governed recreational activities, e.g. role-playing games in which players assume the role of characters in a fictional setting.

schemaClasses.name: GameAvailabilityEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: GameAvailabilityEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: For a [[VideoGame]], such as used with a [[PlayGameAction]], an enumeration of the kind of game availability offered.

schemaClasses.name: GamePlayMode
schemaClasses.label: GamePlayMode
schemaClasses.description: Indicates whether this game is multi-player, co-op or single-player.

schemaClasses.name: GameServer
schemaClasses.label: GameServer
schemaClasses.description: Server that provides game interaction in a multiplayer game.

schemaClasses.name: GameServerStatus
schemaClasses.label: GameServerStatus
schemaClasses.description: Status of a game server.

schemaClasses.name: GardenStore
schemaClasses.label: GardenStore
schemaClasses.description: A garden store.

schemaClasses.name: GasStation
schemaClasses.label: GasStation
schemaClasses.description: A gas station.

schemaClasses.name: GatedResidenceCommunity
schemaClasses.label: GatedResidenceCommunity
schemaClasses.description: Residence type: Gated community.

schemaClasses.name: GenderType
schemaClasses.label: GenderType
schemaClasses.description: An enumeration of genders.

schemaClasses.name: Gene
schemaClasses.label: Gene
schemaClasses.description: A discrete unit of inheritance which affects one or more biological traits (Source: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gene](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gene)). Examples include FOXP2 (Forkhead box protein P2), SCARNA21 (small Cajal body-specific RNA 21), A- (agouti genotype).

schemaClasses.name: GeneralContractor
schemaClasses.label: GeneralContractor
schemaClasses.description: A general contractor.

schemaClasses.name: GeoCircle
schemaClasses.label: GeoCircle
schemaClasses.description:
A GeoCircle is a GeoShape representing a circular geographic area. As it is a GeoShape
          it provides the simple textual property 'circle', but also allows the combination of postalCode alongside geoRadius.
          The center of the circle can be indicated via the 'geoMidpoint' property, or more approximately using 'address', 'postalCode'.

schemaClasses.name: GeoCoordinates
schemaClasses.label: GeoCoordinates
schemaClasses.description: The geographic coordinates of a place or event.

schemaClasses.name: GeoShape
schemaClasses.label: GeoShape
schemaClasses.description: The geographic shape of a place. A GeoShape can be described using several properties whose values are based on latitude/longitude pairs. Either whitespace or commas can be used to separate latitude and longitude; whitespace should be used when writing a list of several such points.

schemaClasses.name: GeospatialGeometry
schemaClasses.label: GeospatialGeometry
schemaClasses.description: (Eventually to be defined as) a supertype of GeoShape designed to accommodate definitions from Geo-Spatial best practices.

schemaClasses.name: GiveAction
schemaClasses.label: GiveAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of transferring ownership of an object to a destination. Reciprocal of TakeAction.

Related actions:

* [[TakeAction]]: Reciprocal of GiveAction.
* [[SendAction]]: Unlike SendAction, GiveAction implies that ownership is being transferred (e.g. I may send my laptop to you, but that doesn't mean I'm giving it to you).
