schemaClasses.name: GolfCourse
schemaClasses.label: GolfCourse
schemaClasses.description: A golf course.

schemaClasses.name: GovernmentBenefitsType
schemaClasses.label: GovernmentBenefitsType
schemaClasses.description: GovernmentBenefitsType enumerates several kinds of government benefits to support the COVID-19 situation. Note that this structure may not capture all benefits offered.

schemaClasses.name: GovernmentBuilding
schemaClasses.label: GovernmentBuilding
schemaClasses.description: A government building.

schemaClasses.name: GovernmentOffice
schemaClasses.label: GovernmentOffice
schemaClasses.description: A government office—for example, an IRS or DMV office.

schemaClasses.name: GovernmentOrganization
schemaClasses.label: GovernmentOrganization
schemaClasses.description: A governmental organization or agency.

schemaClasses.name: GovernmentPermit
schemaClasses.label: GovernmentPermit
schemaClasses.description: A permit issued by a government agency.

schemaClasses.name: GovernmentService
schemaClasses.label: GovernmentService
schemaClasses.description: A service provided by a government organization, e.g. food stamps, veterans benefits, etc.

schemaClasses.name: Grant
schemaClasses.label: Grant
schemaClasses.description:
A grant, typically financial or otherwise quantifiable, of resources. Typically a [[funder]] sponsors some [[MonetaryAmount]] to an [[Organization]] or [[Person]],
    sometimes not necessarily via a dedicated or long-lived [[Project]], resulting in one or more outputs, or [[fundedItem]]s. For financial sponsorship, indicate the [[funder]] of a [[MonetaryGrant]]. For non-financial support, indicate [[sponsor]] of [[Grant]]s of resources (e.g. office space).

Grants support  activities directed towards some agreed collective goals, often but not always organized as [[Project]]s. Long-lived projects are sometimes sponsored by a variety of grants over time, but it is also common for a project to be associated with a single grant.

The amount of a [[Grant]] is represented using [[amount]] as a [[MonetaryAmount]].

schemaClasses.name: GroceryStore
schemaClasses.label: GroceryStore
schemaClasses.description: A grocery store.

schemaClasses.name: Guide
schemaClasses.label: Guide
schemaClasses.description: [[Guide]] is a page or article that recommends specific products or services, or aspects of a thing for a user to consider. A [[Guide]] may represent a Buying Guide and detail aspects of products or services for a user to consider. A [[Guide]] may represent a Product Guide and recommend specific products or services. A [[Guide]] may represent a Ranked List and recommend specific products or services with ranking.

schemaClasses.name: HVACBusiness
schemaClasses.label: HVACBusiness
schemaClasses.description: A business that provides Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning services.

schemaClasses.name: Hackathon
schemaClasses.label: Hackathon
schemaClasses.description: A [hackathon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hackathon) event.

schemaClasses.name: HairSalon
schemaClasses.label: HairSalon
schemaClasses.description: A hair salon.

schemaClasses.name: HardwareStore
schemaClasses.label: HardwareStore
schemaClasses.description: A hardware store.

schemaClasses.name: HealthAndBeautyBusiness
schemaClasses.label: HealthAndBeautyBusiness
schemaClasses.description: Health and beauty.

schemaClasses.name: HealthAspectEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: HealthAspectEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: HealthAspectEnumeration enumerates several aspects of health content online, each of which might be described using [[hasHealthAspect]] and [[HealthTopicContent]].

schemaClasses.name: HealthClub
schemaClasses.label: HealthClub
schemaClasses.description: A health club.

schemaClasses.name: HealthInsurancePlan
schemaClasses.label: HealthInsurancePlan
schemaClasses.description: A US-style health insurance plan, including PPOs, EPOs, and HMOs.

schemaClasses.name: HealthPlanCostSharingSpecification
schemaClasses.label: HealthPlanCostSharingSpecification
schemaClasses.description: A description of costs to the patient under a given network or formulary.

schemaClasses.name: HealthPlanFormulary
schemaClasses.label: HealthPlanFormulary
schemaClasses.description: For a given health insurance plan, the specification for costs and coverage of prescription drugs.

schemaClasses.name: HealthPlanNetwork
schemaClasses.label: HealthPlanNetwork
schemaClasses.description: A US-style health insurance plan network.

schemaClasses.name: HealthTopicContent
schemaClasses.label: HealthTopicContent
schemaClasses.description: [[HealthTopicContent]] is [[WebContent]] that is about some aspect of a health topic, e.g. a condition, its symptoms or treatments. Such content may be comprised of several parts or sections and use different types of media. Multiple instances of [[WebContent]] (and hence [[HealthTopicContent]]) can be related using [[hasPart]] / [[isPartOf]] where there is some kind of content hierarchy, and their content described with [[about]] and [[mentions]] e.g. building upon the existing [[MedicalCondition]] vocabulary.

schemaClasses.name: HighSchool
schemaClasses.label: HighSchool
schemaClasses.description: A high school.

schemaClasses.name: HinduTemple
schemaClasses.label: HinduTemple
schemaClasses.description: A Hindu temple.

schemaClasses.name: HobbyShop
schemaClasses.label: HobbyShop
schemaClasses.description: A store that sells materials useful or necessary for various hobbies.

schemaClasses.name: HomeAndConstructionBusiness
schemaClasses.label: HomeAndConstructionBusiness
schemaClasses.description:
A construction business.

A HomeAndConstructionBusiness is a [[LocalBusiness]] that provides services around homes and buildings.

As a [[LocalBusiness]] it can be described as a [[provider]] of one or more [[Service]]\(s).

schemaClasses.name: HomeGoodsStore
schemaClasses.label: HomeGoodsStore
schemaClasses.description: A home goods store.

schemaClasses.name: Hospital
schemaClasses.label: Hospital
schemaClasses.description: A hospital.

schemaClasses.name: Hostel
schemaClasses.label: Hostel
schemaClasses.description:
A hostel - cheap accommodation, often in shared dormitories.

See also the dedicated document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations.

schemaClasses.name: Hotel
schemaClasses.label: Hotel
schemaClasses.description:
A hotel is an establishment that provides lodging paid on a short-term basis (source: Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia, see http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hotel).

See also the dedicated document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations.

schemaClasses.name: HotelRoom
schemaClasses.label: HotelRoom
schemaClasses.description:
A hotel room is a single room in a hotel.

See also the dedicated document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations.

schemaClasses.name: House
schemaClasses.label: House
schemaClasses.description: A house is a building or structure that has the ability to be occupied for habitation by humans or other creatures (source: Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia, see http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/House).

schemaClasses.name: HousePainter
schemaClasses.label: HousePainter
schemaClasses.description: A house painting service.

schemaClasses.name: HowTo
schemaClasses.label: HowTo
schemaClasses.description: Instructions that explain how to achieve a result by performing a sequence of steps.

schemaClasses.name: HowToDirection
schemaClasses.label: HowToDirection
schemaClasses.description: A direction indicating a single action to do in the instructions for how to achieve a result.

schemaClasses.name: HowToItem
schemaClasses.label: HowToItem
schemaClasses.description: An item used as either a tool or supply when performing the instructions for how to achieve a result.

schemaClasses.name: HowToSection
schemaClasses.label: HowToSection
schemaClasses.description: A sub-grouping of steps in the instructions for how to achieve a result (e.g. steps for making a pie crust within a pie recipe).

schemaClasses.name: HowToStep
schemaClasses.label: HowToStep
schemaClasses.description: A step in the instructions for how to achieve a result. It is an ordered list with HowToDirection and/or HowToTip items.

schemaClasses.name: HowToSupply
schemaClasses.label: HowToSupply
schemaClasses.description: A supply consumed when performing the instructions for how to achieve a result.

schemaClasses.name: HowToTip
schemaClasses.label: HowToTip
schemaClasses.description: An explanation in the instructions for how to achieve a result. It provides supplementary information about a technique, supply, author's preference, etc. It can explain what could be done, or what should not be done, but doesn't specify what should be done (see HowToDirection).

schemaClasses.name: HowToTool
schemaClasses.label: HowToTool
schemaClasses.description: A tool used (but not consumed) when performing instructions for how to achieve a result.

schemaClasses.name: HyperToc
schemaClasses.label: HyperToc
schemaClasses.description: A HyperToc represents a hypertext table of contents for complex media objects, such as [[VideoObject]], [[AudioObject]]. Items in the table of contents are indicated using the [[tocEntry]] property, and typed [[HyperTocEntry]]. For cases where the same larger work is split into multiple files, [[associatedMedia]] can be used on individual [[HyperTocEntry]] items.

schemaClasses.name: HyperTocEntry
schemaClasses.label: HyperTocEntry
schemaClasses.description: A HyperToEntry is an item within a [[HyperToc]], which represents a hypertext table of contents for complex media objects, such as [[VideoObject]], [[AudioObject]]. The media object itself is indicated using [[associatedMedia]]. Each section of interest within that content can be described with a [[HyperTocEntry]], with associated [[startOffset]] and [[endOffset]]. When several entries are all from the same file, [[associatedMedia]] is used on the overarching [[HyperTocEntry]]; if the content has been split into multiple files, they can be referenced using [[associatedMedia]] on each [[HyperTocEntry]].

schemaClasses.name: IPTCDigitalSourceEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: IPTCDigitalSourceEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: IPTC "Digital Source" codes for use with the [[digitalSourceType]] property, providing information about the source for a digital media object. In general these codes are not declared here to be mutually exclusive, although some combinations would be contradictory if applied simultaneously, or might be considered mutually incompatible by upstream maintainers of the definitions. See the IPTC documentation for detailed definitions of all terms.

schemaClasses.name: IceCreamShop
schemaClasses.label: IceCreamShop
schemaClasses.description: An ice cream shop.

schemaClasses.name: IgnoreAction
schemaClasses.label: IgnoreAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of intentionally disregarding the object. An agent ignores an object.

schemaClasses.name: ImageGallery
schemaClasses.label: ImageGallery
schemaClasses.description: Web page type: Image gallery page.

schemaClasses.name: ImageObject
schemaClasses.label: ImageObject
schemaClasses.description: An image file.

schemaClasses.name: ImageObjectSnapshot
schemaClasses.label: ImageObjectSnapshot
schemaClasses.description: A specific and exact (byte-for-byte) version of an [[ImageObject]]. Two byte-for-byte identical files, for the purposes of this type, considered identical. If they have different embedded metadata (e.g. XMP, EXIF) the files will differ. Different external facts about the files, e.g. creator or dateCreated that aren't represented in their actual content, do not affect this notion of identity.

schemaClasses.name: ImagingTest
schemaClasses.label: ImagingTest
schemaClasses.description: Any medical imaging modality typically used for diagnostic purposes.

schemaClasses.name: IncentiveQualifiedExpenseType
schemaClasses.label: IncentiveQualifiedExpenseType
schemaClasses.description: The types of expenses that are covered by the incentive. For example some incentives are only for the goods (tangible items) but the services (labor) are excluded.

schemaClasses.name: IncentiveStatus
schemaClasses.label: IncentiveStatus
schemaClasses.description: Enumerates a status for an incentive, such as whether it is active.

schemaClasses.name: IncentiveType
schemaClasses.label: IncentiveType
schemaClasses.description: Enumerates common financial incentives for products, including tax credits, tax deductions, rebates and subsidies, etc.

schemaClasses.name: IndividualPhysician
schemaClasses.label: IndividualPhysician
schemaClasses.description:
An individual medical practitioner. For their official address use [[address]], for affiliations to hospitals use [[hospitalAffiliation]]. 
The [[practicesAt]] property can be used to indicate [[MedicalOrganization]] hospitals, clinics, pharmacies etc. where this physician practices.

schemaClasses.name: IndividualProduct
schemaClasses.label: IndividualProduct
schemaClasses.description: A single, identifiable product instance (e.g. a laptop with a particular serial number).

schemaClasses.name: InfectiousAgentClass
schemaClasses.label: InfectiousAgentClass
schemaClasses.description: Classes of agents or pathogens that transmit infectious diseases. Enumerated type.

schemaClasses.name: InfectiousDisease
schemaClasses.label: InfectiousDisease
schemaClasses.description: An infectious disease is a clinically evident human disease resulting from the presence of pathogenic microbial agents, like pathogenic viruses, pathogenic bacteria, fungi, protozoa, multicellular parasites, and prions. To be considered an infectious disease, such pathogens are known to be able to cause this disease.

schemaClasses.name: InformAction
schemaClasses.label: InformAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of notifying someone of information pertinent to them, with no expectation of a response.

schemaClasses.name: InsertAction
schemaClasses.label: InsertAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of adding at a specific location in an ordered collection.

schemaClasses.name: InstallAction
schemaClasses.label: InstallAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of installing an application.

schemaClasses.name: InstantaneousEvent
schemaClasses.label: InstantaneousEvent
schemaClasses.description: An event with no duration, like for instance a computer log entry.

schemaClasses.name: InsuranceAgency
schemaClasses.label: InsuranceAgency
schemaClasses.description: An Insurance agency.

schemaClasses.name: Intangible
schemaClasses.label: Intangible
schemaClasses.description: A utility class that serves as the umbrella for a number of 'intangible' things such as quantities, structured values, etc.

schemaClasses.name: Integer
schemaClasses.label: Integer
schemaClasses.description: Data type: Integer.

schemaClasses.name: InteractAction
schemaClasses.label: InteractAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of interacting with another person or organization.

schemaClasses.name: InteractionCounter
schemaClasses.label: InteractionCounter
schemaClasses.description: A summary of how users have interacted with this CreativeWork. In most cases, authors will use a subtype to specify the specific type of interaction.

schemaClasses.name: InternetCafe
schemaClasses.label: InternetCafe
schemaClasses.description: An internet cafe.

schemaClasses.name: InvestmentFund
schemaClasses.label: InvestmentFund
schemaClasses.description: A company or fund that gathers capital from a number of investors to create a pool of money that is then re-invested into stocks, bonds and other assets.

schemaClasses.name: InvestmentOrDeposit
schemaClasses.label: InvestmentOrDeposit
schemaClasses.description: A type of financial product that typically requires the client to transfer funds to a financial service in return for potential beneficial financial return.

schemaClasses.name: InviteAction
schemaClasses.label: InviteAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of asking someone to attend an event. Reciprocal of RsvpAction.

schemaClasses.name: Invoice
schemaClasses.label: Invoice
schemaClasses.description: A statement of the money due for goods or services; a bill.

schemaClasses.name: ItemAvailability
schemaClasses.label: ItemAvailability
schemaClasses.description: A list of possible product availability options.

schemaClasses.name: ItemList
schemaClasses.label: ItemList
schemaClasses.description: A list of items of any sort—for example, Top 10 Movies About Weathermen, or Top 100 Party Songs. Not to be confused with HTML lists, which are often used only for formatting.

schemaClasses.name: ItemListOrderType
schemaClasses.label: ItemListOrderType
schemaClasses.description: Enumerated for values for itemListOrder for indicating how an ordered ItemList is organized.

schemaClasses.name: ItemPage
schemaClasses.label: ItemPage
schemaClasses.description: A page devoted to a single item, such as a particular product or hotel.

schemaClasses.name: JewelryStore
schemaClasses.label: JewelryStore
schemaClasses.description: A jewelry store.

schemaClasses.name: JobPosting
schemaClasses.label: JobPosting
schemaClasses.description: A listing that describes a job opening in a certain organization.

schemaClasses.name: JoinAction
schemaClasses.label: JoinAction
schemaClasses.description:
An agent joins an event/group with participants/friends at a location.

Related actions:

* [[RegisterAction]]: Unlike RegisterAction, JoinAction refers to joining a group/team of people.
* [[SubscribeAction]]: Unlike SubscribeAction, JoinAction does not imply that you'll be receiving updates.
* [[FollowAction]]: Unlike FollowAction, JoinAction does not imply that you'll be polling for updates.

schemaClasses.name: Joint
schemaClasses.label: Joint
schemaClasses.description: The anatomical location at which two or more bones make contact.

schemaClasses.name: LakeBodyOfWater
schemaClasses.label: LakeBodyOfWater
schemaClasses.description: A lake (for example, Lake Pontrachain).

schemaClasses.name: Landform
schemaClasses.label: Landform
schemaClasses.description: A landform or physical feature.  Landform elements include mountains, plains, lakes, rivers, seascape and oceanic waterbody interface features such as bays, peninsulas, seas and so forth, including sub-aqueous terrain features such as submersed mountain ranges, volcanoes, and the great ocean basins.

schemaClasses.name: LandmarksOrHistoricalBuildings
schemaClasses.label: LandmarksOrHistoricalBuildings
schemaClasses.description: An historical landmark or building.

schemaClasses.name: Language
schemaClasses.label: Language
schemaClasses.description: Natural languages such as Spanish, Tamil, Hindi, English, etc. Formal language code tags expressed in [BCP 47](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IETF_language_tag) can be used via the [[alternateName]] property. The Language type previously also covered programming languages such as Scheme and Lisp, which are now best represented using [[ComputerLanguage]].

schemaClasses.name: LearningResource
schemaClasses.label: LearningResource
schemaClasses.description:
The LearningResource type can be used to indicate [[CreativeWork]]s (whether physical or digital) that have a particular and explicit orientation towards learning, education, skill acquisition, and other educational purposes.

[[LearningResource]] is expected to be used as an addition to a primary type such as [[Book]], [[VideoObject]], [[Product]] etc.

[[EducationEvent]] serves a similar purpose for event-like things (e.g. a [[Trip]]). A [[LearningResource]] may be created as a result of an [[EducationEvent]], for example by recording one.

schemaClasses.name: LeaveAction
schemaClasses.label: LeaveAction
schemaClasses.description:
An agent leaves an event / group with participants/friends at a location.

Related actions:

* [[JoinAction]]: The antonym of LeaveAction.
* [[UnRegisterAction]]: Unlike UnRegisterAction, LeaveAction implies leaving a group/team of people rather than a service.

schemaClasses.name: LegalForceStatus
schemaClasses.label: LegalForceStatus
schemaClasses.description: A list of possible statuses for the legal force of a legislation.

schemaClasses.name: LegalService
schemaClasses.label: LegalService
schemaClasses.description: A LegalService is a business that provides legally-oriented services, advice and representation, e.g. law firms.\n\nAs a [[LocalBusiness]] it can be described as a [[provider]] of one or more [[Service]]\(s).

schemaClasses.name: LegalValueLevel
schemaClasses.label: LegalValueLevel
schemaClasses.description: A list of possible levels for the legal validity of a legislation.

schemaClasses.name: Legislation
schemaClasses.label: Legislation
schemaClasses.description: A legal document such as an act, decree, bill, etc. (enforceable or not) or a component of a legal act (like an article).

schemaClasses.name: LegislationObject
schemaClasses.label: LegislationObject
schemaClasses.description: A specific object or file containing a Legislation. Note that the same Legislation can be published in multiple files. For example, a digitally signed PDF, a plain PDF and an HTML version.

schemaClasses.name: LegislativeBuilding
schemaClasses.label: LegislativeBuilding
schemaClasses.description: A legislative building—for example, the state capitol.

schemaClasses.name: LendAction
schemaClasses.label: LendAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of providing an object under an agreement that it will be returned at a later date. Reciprocal of BorrowAction.

Related actions:

* [[BorrowAction]]: Reciprocal of LendAction.

schemaClasses.name: Library
schemaClasses.label: Library
schemaClasses.description: A library.

schemaClasses.name: LibrarySystem
schemaClasses.label: LibrarySystem
schemaClasses.description: A [[LibrarySystem]] is a collaborative system amongst several libraries.

schemaClasses.name: LifestyleModification
schemaClasses.label: LifestyleModification
schemaClasses.description: A process of care involving exercise, changes to diet, fitness routines, and other lifestyle changes aimed at improving a health condition.

schemaClasses.name: Ligament
schemaClasses.label: Ligament
schemaClasses.description: A short band of tough, flexible, fibrous connective tissue that functions to connect multiple bones, cartilages, and structurally support joints.

schemaClasses.name: LikeAction
schemaClasses.label: LikeAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of expressing a positive sentiment about the object. An agent likes an object (a proposition, topic or theme) with participants.

schemaClasses.name: LinkRole
schemaClasses.label: LinkRole
schemaClasses.description: A Role that represents a Web link, e.g. as expressed via the 'url' property. Its linkRelationship property can indicate URL-based and plain textual link types, e.g. those in IANA link registry or others such as 'amphtml'. This structure provides a placeholder where details from HTML's link element can be represented outside of HTML, e.g. in JSON-LD feeds.

schemaClasses.name: LiquorStore
schemaClasses.label: LiquorStore
schemaClasses.description: A shop that sells alcoholic drinks such as wine, beer, whisky and other spirits.

schemaClasses.name: ListItem
schemaClasses.label: ListItem
schemaClasses.description: An list item, e.g. a step in a checklist or how-to description.

schemaClasses.name: ListenAction
schemaClasses.label: ListenAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of consuming audio content.

schemaClasses.name: LiteraryEvent
schemaClasses.label: LiteraryEvent
schemaClasses.description: Event type: Literary event.

schemaClasses.name: LiveBlogPosting
schemaClasses.label: LiveBlogPosting
schemaClasses.description: A [[LiveBlogPosting]] is a [[BlogPosting]] intended to provide a rolling textual coverage of an ongoing event through continuous updates.

schemaClasses.name: LoanOrCredit
schemaClasses.label: LoanOrCredit
schemaClasses.description: A financial product for the loaning of an amount of money, or line of credit, under agreed terms and charges.

schemaClasses.name: LocalBusiness
schemaClasses.label: LocalBusiness
schemaClasses.description: A particular physical business or branch of an organization. Examples of LocalBusiness include a restaurant, a particular branch of a restaurant chain, a branch of a bank, a medical practice, a club, a bowling alley, etc.

schemaClasses.name: LocationFeatureSpecification
schemaClasses.label: LocationFeatureSpecification
schemaClasses.description: Specifies a location feature by providing a structured value representing a feature of an accommodation as a property-value pair of varying degrees of formality.

schemaClasses.name: Locksmith
schemaClasses.label: Locksmith
schemaClasses.description: A locksmith.

schemaClasses.name: LodgingBusiness
schemaClasses.label: LodgingBusiness
schemaClasses.description: A lodging business, such as a motel, hotel, or inn.

schemaClasses.name: LodgingReservation
schemaClasses.label: LodgingReservation
schemaClasses.description: A reservation for lodging at a hotel, motel, inn, etc.\n\nNote: This type is for information about actual reservations, e.g. in confirmation emails or HTML pages with individual confirmations of reservations.

schemaClasses.name: LoginAction
schemaClasses.label: LoginAction
schemaClasses.description: The action of logging into a device or application.

schemaClasses.name: LoseAction
schemaClasses.label: LoseAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of being defeated in a competitive activity.

schemaClasses.name: LymphaticVessel
schemaClasses.label: LymphaticVessel
schemaClasses.description: A type of blood vessel that specifically carries lymph fluid unidirectionally toward the heart.

schemaClasses.name: Manuscript
schemaClasses.label: Manuscript
schemaClasses.description: A book, document, or piece of music written by hand rather than typed or printed.

schemaClasses.name: Map
schemaClasses.label: Map
schemaClasses.description: A map.

schemaClasses.name: MapCategoryType
schemaClasses.label: MapCategoryType
schemaClasses.description: An enumeration of several kinds of Map.

schemaClasses.name: MarryAction
schemaClasses.label: MarryAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of marrying a person.

schemaClasses.name: Mass
schemaClasses.label: Mass
schemaClasses.description: Properties that take Mass as values are of the form '<Number> <Mass unit of measure>'. E.g., '7 kg'.

schemaClasses.name: MathSolver
schemaClasses.label: MathSolver
schemaClasses.description: A math solver which is capable of solving a subset of mathematical problems.

schemaClasses.name: MaximumDoseSchedule
schemaClasses.label: MaximumDoseSchedule
schemaClasses.description: The maximum dosing schedule considered safe for a drug or supplement as recommended by an authority or by the drug/supplement's manufacturer. Capture the recommending authority in the recognizingAuthority property of MedicalEntity.

schemaClasses.name: MeasurementMethodEnum
schemaClasses.label: MeasurementMethodEnum
schemaClasses.description: Enumeration(s) for use with [[measurementMethod]].

schemaClasses.name: MeasurementTypeEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: MeasurementTypeEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Enumeration of common measurement types (or dimensions), for example "chest" for a person, "inseam" for pants, "gauge" for screws, or "wheel" for bicycles.

schemaClasses.name: MediaEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: MediaEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: MediaEnumeration enumerations are lists of codes, labels etc. useful for describing media objects. They may be reflections of externally developed lists, or created at schema.org, or a combination.

schemaClasses.name: MediaGallery
schemaClasses.label: MediaGallery
schemaClasses.description: Web page type: Media gallery page. A mixed-media page that can contain media such as images, videos, and other multimedia.

schemaClasses.name: MediaManipulationRatingEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: MediaManipulationRatingEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Codes for use with the [[mediaAuthenticityCategory]] property, indicating the authenticity of a media object (in the context of how it was published or shared). In general these codes are not mutually exclusive, although some combinations (such as 'original' versus 'transformed', 'edited' and 'staged') would be contradictory if applied in the same [[MediaReview]]. Note that the application of these codes is with regard to a piece of media shared or published in a particular context.

schemaClasses.name: MediaObject
schemaClasses.label: MediaObject
schemaClasses.description: A media object, such as an image, video, audio, or text object embedded in a web page or a downloadable dataset i.e. DataDownload. Note that a creative work may have many media objects associated with it on the same web page. For example, a page about a single song (MusicRecording) may have a music video (VideoObject), and a high and low bandwidth audio stream (2 AudioObject's).

schemaClasses.name: MediaReview
schemaClasses.label: MediaReview
schemaClasses.description:
A [[MediaReview]] is a more specialized form of Review dedicated to the evaluation of media content online, typically in the context of fact-checking and misinformation.
    For more general reviews of media in the broader sense, use [[UserReview]], [[CriticReview]] or other [[Review]] types. This definition is
    a work in progress. While the [[MediaManipulationRatingEnumeration]] list reflects significant community review amongst fact-checkers and others working
    to combat misinformation, the specific structures for representing media objects, their versions and publication context, are still evolving. Similarly, best practices for the relationship between [[MediaReview]] and [[ClaimReview]] markup have not yet been finalized.

schemaClasses.name: MediaReviewItem
schemaClasses.label: MediaReviewItem
schemaClasses.description: Represents an item or group of closely related items treated as a unit for the sake of evaluation in a [[MediaReview]]. Authorship etc. apply to the items rather than to the curation/grouping or reviewing party.

schemaClasses.name: MediaSubscription
schemaClasses.label: MediaSubscription
schemaClasses.description: A subscription which allows a user to access media including audio, video, books, etc.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalAudience
schemaClasses.label: MedicalAudience
schemaClasses.description: Target audiences for medical web pages.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalAudienceType
schemaClasses.label: MedicalAudienceType
schemaClasses.description: Target audiences types for medical web pages. Enumerated type.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalBusiness
schemaClasses.label: MedicalBusiness
schemaClasses.description: A particular physical or virtual business of an organization for medical purposes. Examples of MedicalBusiness include different businesses run by health professionals.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalCause
schemaClasses.label: MedicalCause
schemaClasses.description: The causative agent(s) that are responsible for the pathophysiologic process that eventually results in a medical condition, symptom or sign. In this schema, unless otherwise specified this is meant to be the proximate cause of the medical condition, symptom or sign. The proximate cause is defined as the causative agent that most directly results in the medical condition, symptom or sign. For example, the HIV virus could be considered a cause of AIDS. Or in a diagnostic context, if a patient fell and sustained a hip fracture and two days later sustained a pulmonary embolism which eventuated in a cardiac arrest, the cause of the cardiac arrest (the proximate cause) would be the pulmonary embolism and not the fall. Medical causes can include cardiovascular, chemical, dermatologic, endocrine, environmental, gastroenterologic, genetic, hematologic, gynecologic, iatrogenic, infectious, musculoskeletal, neurologic, nutritional, obstetric, oncologic, otolaryngologic, pharmacologic, psychiatric, pulmonary, renal, rheumatologic, toxic, traumatic, or urologic causes; medical conditions can be causes as well.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalClinic
schemaClasses.label: MedicalClinic
schemaClasses.description: A facility, often associated with a hospital or medical school, that is devoted to the specific diagnosis and/or healthcare. Previously limited to outpatients but with evolution it may be open to inpatients as well.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalCode
schemaClasses.label: MedicalCode
schemaClasses.description: A code for a medical entity.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalCondition
schemaClasses.label: MedicalCondition
schemaClasses.description: Any condition of the human body that affects the normal functioning of a person, whether physically or mentally. Includes diseases, injuries, disabilities, disorders, syndromes, etc.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalConditionStage
schemaClasses.label: MedicalConditionStage
schemaClasses.description: A stage of a medical condition, such as 'Stage IIIa'.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalContraindication
schemaClasses.label: MedicalContraindication
schemaClasses.description: A condition or factor that serves as a reason to withhold a certain medical therapy. Contraindications can be absolute (there are no reasonable circumstances for undertaking a course of action) or relative (the patient is at higher risk of complications, but these risks may be outweighed by other considerations or mitigated by other measures).

schemaClasses.name: MedicalDevice
schemaClasses.label: MedicalDevice
schemaClasses.description: Any object used in a medical capacity, such as to diagnose or treat a patient.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalDevicePurpose
schemaClasses.label: MedicalDevicePurpose
schemaClasses.description: Categories of medical devices, organized by the purpose or intended use of the device.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalEntity
schemaClasses.label: MedicalEntity
schemaClasses.description: The most generic type of entity related to health and the practice of medicine.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: MedicalEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Enumerations related to health and the practice of medicine: A concept that is used to attribute a quality to another concept, as a qualifier, a collection of items or a listing of all of the elements of a set in medicine practice.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalEvidenceLevel
schemaClasses.label: MedicalEvidenceLevel
schemaClasses.description: Level of evidence for a medical guideline. Enumerated type.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalGuideline
schemaClasses.label: MedicalGuideline
schemaClasses.description: Any recommendation made by a standard society (e.g. ACC/AHA) or consensus statement that denotes how to diagnose and treat a particular condition. Note: this type should be used to tag the actual guideline recommendation; if the guideline recommendation occurs in a larger scholarly article, use MedicalScholarlyArticle to tag the overall article, not this type. Note also: the organization making the recommendation should be captured in the recognizingAuthority base property of MedicalEntity.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalGuidelineContraindication
schemaClasses.label: MedicalGuidelineContraindication
schemaClasses.description: A guideline contraindication that designates a process as harmful and where quality of the data supporting the contraindication is sound.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalGuidelineRecommendation
schemaClasses.label: MedicalGuidelineRecommendation
schemaClasses.description: A guideline recommendation that is regarded as efficacious and where quality of the data supporting the recommendation is sound.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalImagingTechnique
schemaClasses.label: MedicalImagingTechnique
schemaClasses.description: Any medical imaging modality typically used for diagnostic purposes. Enumerated type.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalIndication
schemaClasses.label: MedicalIndication
schemaClasses.description: A condition or factor that indicates use of a medical therapy, including signs, symptoms, risk factors, anatomical states, etc.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalIntangible
schemaClasses.label: MedicalIntangible
schemaClasses.description: A utility class that serves as the umbrella for a number of 'intangible' things in the medical space.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalObservationalStudy
schemaClasses.label: MedicalObservationalStudy
schemaClasses.description: An observational study is a type of medical study that attempts to infer the possible effect of a treatment through observation of a cohort of subjects over a period of time. In an observational study, the assignment of subjects into treatment groups versus control groups is outside the control of the investigator. This is in contrast with controlled studies, such as the randomized controlled trials represented by MedicalTrial, where each subject is randomly assigned to a treatment group or a control group before the start of the treatment.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalObservationalStudyDesign
schemaClasses.label: MedicalObservationalStudyDesign
schemaClasses.description: Design models for observational medical studies. Enumerated type.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalOrganization
schemaClasses.label: MedicalOrganization
schemaClasses.description: A medical organization (physical or not), such as hospital, institution or clinic.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalProcedure
schemaClasses.label: MedicalProcedure
schemaClasses.description: A process of care used in either a diagnostic, therapeutic, preventive or palliative capacity that relies on invasive (surgical), non-invasive, or other techniques.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalProcedureType
schemaClasses.label: MedicalProcedureType
schemaClasses.description: An enumeration that describes different types of medical procedures.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalRiskCalculator
schemaClasses.label: MedicalRiskCalculator
schemaClasses.description: A complex mathematical calculation requiring an online calculator, used to assess prognosis. Note: use the url property of Thing to record any URLs for online calculators.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalRiskEstimator
schemaClasses.label: MedicalRiskEstimator
schemaClasses.description: Any rule set or interactive tool for estimating the risk of developing a complication or condition.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalRiskFactor
schemaClasses.label: MedicalRiskFactor
schemaClasses.description: A risk factor is anything that increases a person's likelihood of developing or contracting a disease, medical condition, or complication.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalRiskScore
schemaClasses.label: MedicalRiskScore
schemaClasses.description: A simple system that adds up the number of risk factors to yield a score that is associated with prognosis, e.g. CHAD score, TIMI risk score.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalScholarlyArticle
schemaClasses.label: MedicalScholarlyArticle
schemaClasses.description: A scholarly article in the medical domain.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalSign
schemaClasses.label: MedicalSign
schemaClasses.description: Any physical manifestation of a person's medical condition discoverable by objective diagnostic tests or physical examination.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalSignOrSymptom
schemaClasses.label: MedicalSignOrSymptom
schemaClasses.description: Any feature associated or not with a medical condition. In medicine a symptom is generally subjective while a sign is objective.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalSpecialty
schemaClasses.label: MedicalSpecialty
schemaClasses.description: Any specific branch of medical science or practice. Medical specialities include clinical specialties that pertain to particular organ systems and their respective disease states, as well as allied health specialties. Enumerated type.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalStudy
schemaClasses.label: MedicalStudy
schemaClasses.description: A medical study is an umbrella type covering all kinds of research studies relating to human medicine or health, including observational studies and interventional trials and registries, randomized, controlled or not. When the specific type of study is known, use one of the extensions of this type, such as MedicalTrial or MedicalObservationalStudy. Also, note that this type should be used to mark up data that describes the study itself; to tag an article that publishes the results of a study, use MedicalScholarlyArticle. Note: use the code property of MedicalEntity to store study IDs, e.g. clinicaltrials.gov ID.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalStudyStatus
schemaClasses.label: MedicalStudyStatus
schemaClasses.description: The status of a medical study. Enumerated type.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalSymptom
schemaClasses.label: MedicalSymptom
schemaClasses.description: Any complaint sensed and expressed by the patient (therefore defined as subjective)  like stomachache, lower-back pain, or fatigue.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalTest
schemaClasses.label: MedicalTest
schemaClasses.description: Any medical test, typically performed for diagnostic purposes.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalTestPanel
schemaClasses.label: MedicalTestPanel
schemaClasses.description: Any collection of tests commonly ordered together.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalTherapy
schemaClasses.label: MedicalTherapy
schemaClasses.description: Any medical intervention designed to prevent, treat, and cure human diseases and medical conditions, including both curative and palliative therapies. Medical therapies are typically processes of care relying upon pharmacotherapy, behavioral therapy, supportive therapy (with fluid or nutrition for example), or detoxification (e.g. hemodialysis) aimed at improving or preventing a health condition.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalTrial
schemaClasses.label: MedicalTrial
schemaClasses.description: A medical trial is a type of medical study that uses a scientific process to compare the safety and efficacy of medical therapies or medical procedures. In general, medical trials are controlled and subjects are allocated at random to the different treatment and/or control groups.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalTrialDesign
schemaClasses.label: MedicalTrialDesign
schemaClasses.description: Design models for medical trials. Enumerated type.

schemaClasses.name: MedicalWebPage
schemaClasses.label: MedicalWebPage
schemaClasses.description: A web page that provides medical information.

schemaClasses.name: MedicineSystem
schemaClasses.label: MedicineSystem
schemaClasses.description: Systems of medical practice.

schemaClasses.name: MeetingRoom
schemaClasses.label: MeetingRoom
schemaClasses.description:
A meeting room, conference room, or conference hall is a room provided for singular events such as business conferences and meetings (source: Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia, see http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conference_hall).

See also the dedicated document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations.

schemaClasses.name: MemberProgram
schemaClasses.label: MemberProgram
schemaClasses.description: A MemberProgram defines a loyalty (or membership) program that provides its members with certain benefits, for example better pricing, free shipping or returns, or the ability to earn loyalty points. Member programs may have multiple tiers, for example silver and gold members, each with different benefits.

schemaClasses.name: MemberProgramTier
schemaClasses.label: MemberProgramTier
schemaClasses.description: A MemberProgramTier specifies a tier under a loyalty (member) program, for example "gold".

schemaClasses.name: MensClothingStore
schemaClasses.label: MensClothingStore
schemaClasses.description: A men's clothing store.

schemaClasses.name: Menu
schemaClasses.label: Menu
schemaClasses.description: A structured representation of food or drink items available from a FoodEstablishment.

schemaClasses.name: MenuItem
schemaClasses.label: MenuItem
schemaClasses.description: A food or drink item listed in a menu or menu section.

schemaClasses.name: MenuSection
schemaClasses.label: MenuSection
schemaClasses.description: A sub-grouping of food or drink items in a menu. E.g. courses (such as 'Dinner', 'Breakfast', etc.), specific type of dishes (such as 'Meat', 'Vegan', 'Drinks', etc.), or some other classification made by the menu provider.

schemaClasses.name: MerchantReturnEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: MerchantReturnEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Enumerates several kinds of product return policies.

schemaClasses.name: MerchantReturnPolicy
schemaClasses.label: MerchantReturnPolicy
schemaClasses.description: A MerchantReturnPolicy provides information about product return policies associated with an [[Organization]], [[Product]], or [[Offer]].

schemaClasses.name: MerchantReturnPolicySeasonalOverride
schemaClasses.label: MerchantReturnPolicySeasonalOverride
schemaClasses.description: A seasonal override of a return policy, for example used for holidays.

schemaClasses.name: Message
schemaClasses.label: Message
schemaClasses.description: A single message from a sender to one or more organizations or people.

schemaClasses.name: MiddleSchool
schemaClasses.label: MiddleSchool
schemaClasses.description: A middle school (typically for children aged around 11-14, although this varies somewhat).

schemaClasses.name: MobileApplication
schemaClasses.label: MobileApplication
schemaClasses.description: A software application designed specifically to work well on a mobile device such as a telephone.

schemaClasses.name: MobilePhoneStore
schemaClasses.label: MobilePhoneStore
schemaClasses.description: A store that sells mobile phones and related accessories.

schemaClasses.name: MolecularEntity
schemaClasses.label: MolecularEntity
schemaClasses.description: Any constitutionally or isotopically distinct atom, molecule, ion, ion pair, radical, radical ion, complex, conformer etc., identifiable as a separately distinguishable entity.

schemaClasses.name: MonetaryAmount
schemaClasses.label: MonetaryAmount
schemaClasses.description: A monetary value or range. This type can be used to describe an amount of money such as $50 USD, or a range as in describing a bank account being suitable for a balance between £1,000 and £1,000,000 GBP, or the value of a salary, etc. It is recommended to use [[PriceSpecification]] Types to describe the price of an Offer, Invoice, etc.

schemaClasses.name: MonetaryAmountDistribution
schemaClasses.label: MonetaryAmountDistribution
schemaClasses.description: A statistical distribution of monetary amounts.

schemaClasses.name: MonetaryGrant
schemaClasses.label: MonetaryGrant
schemaClasses.description: A monetary grant.

schemaClasses.name: MoneyTransfer
schemaClasses.label: MoneyTransfer
schemaClasses.description: The act of transferring money from one place to another place. This may occur electronically or physically.

schemaClasses.name: MortgageLoan
schemaClasses.label: MortgageLoan
schemaClasses.description: A loan in which property or real estate is used as collateral. (A loan securitized against some real estate.)

schemaClasses.name: Mosque
schemaClasses.label: Mosque
schemaClasses.description: A mosque.

schemaClasses.name: Motel
schemaClasses.label: Motel
schemaClasses.description:
A motel.

See also the dedicated document on the use of schema.org for marking up hotels and other forms of accommodations.

schemaClasses.name: Motorcycle
schemaClasses.label: Motorcycle
schemaClasses.description: A motorcycle or motorbike is a single-track, two-wheeled motor vehicle.

schemaClasses.name: MotorcycleDealer
schemaClasses.label: MotorcycleDealer
schemaClasses.description: A motorcycle dealer.

schemaClasses.name: MotorcycleRepair
schemaClasses.label: MotorcycleRepair
schemaClasses.description: A motorcycle repair shop.

schemaClasses.name: MotorizedBicycle
schemaClasses.label: MotorizedBicycle
schemaClasses.description: A motorized bicycle is a bicycle with an attached motor used to power the vehicle, or to assist with pedaling.

schemaClasses.name: Mountain
schemaClasses.label: Mountain
schemaClasses.description: A mountain, like Mount Whitney or Mount Everest.

schemaClasses.name: MoveAction
schemaClasses.label: MoveAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of an agent relocating to a place.

Related actions:

* [[TransferAction]]: Unlike TransferAction, the subject of the move is a living Person or Organization rather than an inanimate object.

schemaClasses.name: Movie
schemaClasses.label: Movie
schemaClasses.description: A movie.

schemaClasses.name: MovieClip
schemaClasses.label: MovieClip
schemaClasses.description: A short segment/part of a movie.

schemaClasses.name: MovieRentalStore
schemaClasses.label: MovieRentalStore
schemaClasses.description: A movie rental store.

schemaClasses.name: MovieSeries
schemaClasses.label: MovieSeries
schemaClasses.description: A series of movies. Included movies can be indicated with the hasPart property.

schemaClasses.name: MovieTheater
schemaClasses.label: MovieTheater
schemaClasses.description: A movie theater.

schemaClasses.name: MovingCompany
schemaClasses.label: MovingCompany
schemaClasses.description: A moving company.

schemaClasses.name: Muscle
schemaClasses.label: Muscle
schemaClasses.description: A muscle is an anatomical structure consisting of a contractile form of tissue that animals use to effect movement.

schemaClasses.name: Museum
schemaClasses.label: Museum
schemaClasses.description: A museum.

schemaClasses.name: MusicAlbum
schemaClasses.label: MusicAlbum
schemaClasses.description: A collection of music tracks.

schemaClasses.name: MusicAlbumProductionType
schemaClasses.label: MusicAlbumProductionType
schemaClasses.description: Classification of the album by its type of content: soundtrack, live album, studio album, etc.

schemaClasses.name: MusicAlbumReleaseType
schemaClasses.label: MusicAlbumReleaseType
schemaClasses.description: The kind of release which this album is: single, EP or album.

schemaClasses.name: MusicComposition
schemaClasses.label: MusicComposition
schemaClasses.description: A musical composition.

schemaClasses.name: MusicEvent
schemaClasses.label: MusicEvent
schemaClasses.description: Event type: Music event.

schemaClasses.name: MusicGroup
schemaClasses.label: MusicGroup
schemaClasses.description: A musical group, such as a band, an orchestra, or a choir. Can also be a solo musician.

schemaClasses.name: MusicPlaylist
schemaClasses.label: MusicPlaylist
schemaClasses.description: A collection of music tracks in playlist form.

schemaClasses.name: MusicRecording
schemaClasses.label: MusicRecording
schemaClasses.description: A music recording (track), usually a single song.

schemaClasses.name: MusicRelease
schemaClasses.label: MusicRelease
schemaClasses.description: A MusicRelease is a specific release of a music album.

schemaClasses.name: MusicReleaseFormatType
schemaClasses.label: MusicReleaseFormatType
schemaClasses.description: Format of this release (the type of recording media used, i.e. compact disc, digital media, LP, etc.).

schemaClasses.name: MusicStore
schemaClasses.label: MusicStore
schemaClasses.description: A music store.

schemaClasses.name: MusicVenue
schemaClasses.label: MusicVenue
schemaClasses.description: A music venue.

schemaClasses.name: MusicVideoObject
schemaClasses.label: MusicVideoObject
schemaClasses.description: A music video file.

schemaClasses.name: NGO
schemaClasses.label: NGO
schemaClasses.description: Organization: Non-governmental Organization.

schemaClasses.name: NLNonprofitType
schemaClasses.label: NLNonprofitType
schemaClasses.description: NLNonprofitType: Non-profit organization type originating from the Netherlands.

schemaClasses.name: NailSalon
schemaClasses.label: NailSalon
schemaClasses.description: A nail salon.

schemaClasses.name: Nerve
schemaClasses.label: Nerve
schemaClasses.description: A common pathway for the electrochemical nerve impulses that are transmitted along each of the axons.

schemaClasses.name: NewsArticle
schemaClasses.label: NewsArticle
schemaClasses.description:
A NewsArticle is an article whose content reports news, or provides background context and supporting materials for understanding the news.

A more detailed overview of [schema.org News markup](/docs/news.html) is also available.

schemaClasses.name: NewsMediaOrganization
schemaClasses.label: NewsMediaOrganization
schemaClasses.description: A News/Media organization such as a newspaper or TV station.

schemaClasses.name: Newspaper
schemaClasses.label: Newspaper
schemaClasses.description: A publication containing information about varied topics that are pertinent to general information, a geographic area, or a specific subject matter (i.e. business, culture, education). Often published daily.

schemaClasses.name: NightClub
schemaClasses.label: NightClub
schemaClasses.description: A nightclub or discotheque.

schemaClasses.name: NonprofitType
schemaClasses.label: NonprofitType
schemaClasses.description: NonprofitType enumerates several kinds of official non-profit types of which a non-profit organization can be.

schemaClasses.name: Notary
schemaClasses.label: Notary
schemaClasses.description: A notary.

schemaClasses.name: NoteDigitalDocument
schemaClasses.label: NoteDigitalDocument
schemaClasses.description: A file containing a note, primarily for the author.

schemaClasses.name: Number
schemaClasses.label: Number
schemaClasses.description:
Data type: Number.

Usage guidelines:

* Use values from 0123456789 (Unicode 'DIGIT ZERO' (U+0030) to 'DIGIT NINE' (U+0039)) rather than superficially similar Unicode symbols.
* Use '.' (Unicode 'FULL STOP' (U+002E)) rather than ',' to indicate a decimal point. Avoid using these symbols as a readability separator.

schemaClasses.name: NutritionInformation
schemaClasses.label: NutritionInformation
schemaClasses.description: Nutritional information about the recipe.

schemaClasses.name: Observation
schemaClasses.label: Observation
schemaClasses.description:
Instances of the class [[Observation]] are used to specify observations about an entity at a particular time. The principal properties of an [[Observation]] are [[observationAbout]], [[measuredProperty]], [[statType]], [[value] and [[observationDate]]  and [[measuredProperty]]. Some but not all Observations represent a [[QuantitativeValue]]. Quantitative observations can be about a [[StatisticalVariable]], which is an abstract specification about which we can make observations that are grounded at a particular location and time.

Observations can also encode a subset of simple RDF-like statements (its observationAbout, a StatisticalVariable, defining the measuredPoperty; its observationAbout property indicating the entity the statement is about, and [[value]] )

In the context of a quantitative knowledge graph, typical properties could include [[measuredProperty]], [[observationAbout]], [[observationDate]], [[value]], [[unitCode]], [[unitText]], [[measurementMethod]].

schemaClasses.name: Occupation
schemaClasses.label: Occupation
schemaClasses.description: A profession, may involve prolonged training and/or a formal qualification.

schemaClasses.name: OccupationalExperienceRequirements
schemaClasses.label: OccupationalExperienceRequirements
schemaClasses.description: Indicates employment-related experience requirements, e.g. [[monthsOfExperience]].

schemaClasses.name: OccupationalTherapy
schemaClasses.label: OccupationalTherapy
schemaClasses.description: A treatment of people with physical, emotional, or social problems, using purposeful activity to help them overcome or learn to deal with their problems.

schemaClasses.name: OceanBodyOfWater
schemaClasses.label: OceanBodyOfWater
schemaClasses.description: An ocean (for example, the Pacific).

schemaClasses.name: Offer
schemaClasses.label: Offer
schemaClasses.description:
An offer to transfer some rights to an item or to provide a service — for example, an offer to sell tickets to an event, to rent the DVD of a movie, to stream a TV show over the internet, to repair a motorcycle, or to loan a book.

Note: As the [[businessFunction]] property, which identifies the form of offer (e.g. sell, lease, repair, dispose), defaults to http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Sell; an Offer without a defined businessFunction value can be assumed to be an offer to sell.

For [GTIN](http://www.gs1.org/barcodes/technical/idkeys/gtin)-related fields, see [Check Digit calculator](http://www.gs1.org/barcodes/support/check_digit_calculator) and [validation guide](http://www.gs1us.org/resources/standards/gtin-validation-guide) from [GS1](http://www.gs1.org/).

schemaClasses.name: OfferCatalog
schemaClasses.label: OfferCatalog
schemaClasses.description: An OfferCatalog is an ItemList that contains related Offers and/or further OfferCatalogs that are offeredBy the same provider.

schemaClasses.name: OfferForLease
schemaClasses.label: OfferForLease
schemaClasses.description:
An [[OfferForLease]] in Schema.org represents an [[Offer]] to lease out something, i.e. an [[Offer]] whose
  [[businessFunction]] is [lease out](http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#LeaseOut.). See [Good Relations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GoodRelations) for
  background on the underlying concepts.

schemaClasses.name: OfferForPurchase
schemaClasses.label: OfferForPurchase
schemaClasses.description:
An [[OfferForPurchase]] in Schema.org represents an [[Offer]] to sell something, i.e. an [[Offer]] whose
  [[businessFunction]] is [sell](http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Sell.). See [Good Relations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GoodRelations) for
  background on the underlying concepts.

schemaClasses.name: OfferItemCondition
schemaClasses.label: OfferItemCondition
schemaClasses.description: A list of possible conditions for the item.

schemaClasses.name: OfferShippingDetails
schemaClasses.label: OfferShippingDetails
schemaClasses.description:
OfferShippingDetails represents information about shipping destinations.

Multiple of these entities can be used to represent different shipping rates for different destinations:

One entity for Alaska/Hawaii. A different one for continental US. A different one for all France.

Multiple of these entities can be used to represent different shipping costs and delivery times.

Two entities that are identical but differ in rate and time:

E.g. Cheaper and slower: $5 in 5-7 days
or Fast and expensive: $15 in 1-2 days.

schemaClasses.name: OfficeEquipmentStore
schemaClasses.label: OfficeEquipmentStore
schemaClasses.description: An office equipment store.

schemaClasses.name: OnDemandEvent
schemaClasses.label: OnDemandEvent
schemaClasses.description: A publication event, e.g. catch-up TV or radio podcast, during which a program is available on-demand.

schemaClasses.name: OnlineBusiness
schemaClasses.label: OnlineBusiness
schemaClasses.description: A particular online business, either standalone or the online part of a broader organization. Examples include an eCommerce site, an online travel booking site, an online learning site, an online logistics and shipping provider, an online (virtual) doctor, etc.

schemaClasses.name: OnlineMarketplace
schemaClasses.label: OnlineMarketplace
schemaClasses.description: An eCommerce marketplace.

schemaClasses.name: OnlineStore
schemaClasses.label: OnlineStore
schemaClasses.description: An eCommerce site.

schemaClasses.name: OpeningHoursSpecification
schemaClasses.label: OpeningHoursSpecification
schemaClasses.description:
A structured value providing information about the opening hours of a place or a certain service inside a place.


The place is __open__ if the [[opens]] property is specified, and __closed__ otherwise.

If the value for the [[closes]] property is less than the value for the [[opens]] property then the hour range is assumed to span over the next day.

schemaClasses.name: OperatingSystem
schemaClasses.label: OperatingSystem
schemaClasses.description: System software that manages computer hardware and software resources, and provides common services for computer programs.

schemaClasses.name: OpinionNewsArticle
schemaClasses.label: OpinionNewsArticle
schemaClasses.description: An [[OpinionNewsArticle]] is a [[NewsArticle]] that primarily expresses opinions rather than journalistic reporting of news and events. For example, a [[NewsArticle]] consisting of a column or [[Blog]]/[[BlogPosting]] entry in the Opinions section of a news publication.

schemaClasses.name: Optician
schemaClasses.label: Optician
schemaClasses.description: A store that sells reading glasses and similar devices for improving vision.

schemaClasses.name: Order
schemaClasses.label: Order
schemaClasses.description: An order is a confirmation of a transaction (a receipt), which can contain multiple line items, each represented by an Offer that has been accepted by the customer.

schemaClasses.name: OrderAction
schemaClasses.label: OrderAction
schemaClasses.description: An agent orders an object/product/service to be delivered/sent.

schemaClasses.name: OrderItem
schemaClasses.label: OrderItem
schemaClasses.description: An order item is a line of an order. It includes the quantity and shipping details of a bought offer.

schemaClasses.name: OrderStatus
schemaClasses.label: OrderStatus
schemaClasses.description: Enumerated status values for Order.

schemaClasses.name: Organization
schemaClasses.label: Organization
schemaClasses.description: An organization such as a school, NGO, corporation, club, etc.

schemaClasses.name: OrganizationRole
schemaClasses.label: OrganizationRole
schemaClasses.description: A subclass of Role used to describe roles within organizations.

schemaClasses.name: OrganizeAction
schemaClasses.label: OrganizeAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of manipulating/administering/supervising/controlling one or more objects.

schemaClasses.name: OutletStore
schemaClasses.label: OutletStore
schemaClasses.description: An outlet store.

schemaClasses.name: OwnershipInfo
schemaClasses.label: OwnershipInfo
schemaClasses.description: A structured value providing information about when a certain organization or person owned a certain product.

schemaClasses.name: PaintAction
schemaClasses.label: PaintAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of producing a painting, typically with paint and canvas as instruments.

schemaClasses.name: Painting
schemaClasses.label: Painting
schemaClasses.description: A painting.

schemaClasses.name: PalliativeProcedure
schemaClasses.label: PalliativeProcedure
schemaClasses.description: A medical procedure intended primarily for palliative purposes, aimed at relieving the symptoms of an underlying health condition.

schemaClasses.name: ParcelDelivery
schemaClasses.label: ParcelDelivery
schemaClasses.description: The delivery of a parcel either via the postal service or a commercial service.

schemaClasses.name: ParentAudience
schemaClasses.label: ParentAudience
schemaClasses.description: A set of characteristics describing parents, who can be interested in viewing some content.

schemaClasses.name: Park
schemaClasses.label: Park
schemaClasses.description: A park.

schemaClasses.name: ParkingFacility
schemaClasses.label: ParkingFacility
schemaClasses.description: A parking lot or other parking facility.

schemaClasses.name: PathologyTest
schemaClasses.label: PathologyTest
schemaClasses.description: A medical test performed by a laboratory that typically involves examination of a tissue sample by a pathologist.

schemaClasses.name: Patient
schemaClasses.label: Patient
schemaClasses.description: A patient is any person recipient of health care services.

schemaClasses.name: PawnShop
schemaClasses.label: PawnShop
schemaClasses.description: A shop that will buy, or lend money against the security of, personal possessions.

schemaClasses.name: PayAction
schemaClasses.label: PayAction
schemaClasses.description: An agent pays a price to a participant.

schemaClasses.name: PaymentCard
schemaClasses.label: PaymentCard
schemaClasses.description: A payment method using a credit, debit, store or other card to associate the payment with an account.

schemaClasses.name: PaymentChargeSpecification
schemaClasses.label: PaymentChargeSpecification
schemaClasses.description: The costs of settling the payment using a particular payment method.

schemaClasses.name: PaymentMethod
schemaClasses.label: PaymentMethod
schemaClasses.description:
A payment method is a standardized procedure for transferring the monetary amount for a purchase. Payment methods are characterized by the legal and technical structures used, and by the organization or group carrying out the transaction. The following legacy values should be accepted: 

* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#ByBankTransferInAdvance
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#ByInvoice
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#Cash
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#CheckInAdvance
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#COD
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#DirectDebit
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#GoogleCheckout
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#PayPal
* http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#PaySwarm

Structured values, or [UNCE payment means](https://vocabulary.uncefact.org/PaymentMeans) are recommended or for newer annotations.

schemaClasses.name: PaymentMethodType
schemaClasses.label: PaymentMethodType
schemaClasses.description: The type of payment method, only for generic payment types, specific forms of payments, like card payment should be expressed using subclasses of PaymentMethod.

schemaClasses.name: PaymentService
schemaClasses.label: PaymentService
schemaClasses.description: A Service to transfer funds from a person or organization to a beneficiary person or organization.

schemaClasses.name: PaymentStatusType
schemaClasses.label: PaymentStatusType
schemaClasses.description: A specific payment status. For example, PaymentDue, PaymentComplete, etc.

schemaClasses.name: PeopleAudience
schemaClasses.label: PeopleAudience
schemaClasses.description: A set of characteristics belonging to people, e.g. who compose an item's target audience.

schemaClasses.name: PerformAction
schemaClasses.label: PerformAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of participating in performance arts.

schemaClasses.name: PerformanceRole
schemaClasses.label: PerformanceRole
schemaClasses.description: A PerformanceRole is a Role that some entity places with regard to a theatrical performance, e.g. in a Movie, TVSeries etc.

schemaClasses.name: PerformingArtsEvent
schemaClasses.label: PerformingArtsEvent
schemaClasses.description:
Live performance Event of the performing arts (music, theatre, dance, acrobatics, spoken word), including performance art and performative sports (e.g. choreographed forms of martial arts, figure skating, competitive ballroom dancing).

Note: Use additionalType to differentiate between productions / shows (PerformanceWork, EventSeries), tours (EventSeries), and individual performances.

schemaClasses.name: PerformingArtsTheater
schemaClasses.label: PerformingArtsTheater
schemaClasses.description: A theater or other performing art center.

schemaClasses.name: PerformingGroup
schemaClasses.label: PerformingGroup
schemaClasses.description: A performance group, such as a band, an orchestra, or a circus.

schemaClasses.name: Periodical
schemaClasses.label: Periodical
schemaClasses.description: A publication in any medium issued in successive parts bearing numerical or chronological designations and intended to continue indefinitely, such as a magazine, scholarly journal, or newspaper.\n\nSee also [blog post](https://blog.schema.org/2014/09/02/schema-org-support-for-bibliographic-relationships-and-periodicals/).

schemaClasses.name: Permit
schemaClasses.label: Permit
schemaClasses.description: A permit issued by an organization, e.g. a parking pass.

schemaClasses.name: Person
schemaClasses.label: Person
schemaClasses.description: A person (alive, dead, undead, or fictional).

schemaClasses.name: PetStore
schemaClasses.label: PetStore
schemaClasses.description: A pet store.

schemaClasses.name: Pharmacy
schemaClasses.label: Pharmacy
schemaClasses.description: A pharmacy or drugstore.

schemaClasses.name: Photograph
schemaClasses.label: Photograph
schemaClasses.description: A photograph.

schemaClasses.name: PhotographAction
schemaClasses.label: PhotographAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of capturing still images of objects using a camera.

schemaClasses.name: PhysicalActivity
schemaClasses.label: PhysicalActivity
schemaClasses.description: Any bodily activity that enhances or maintains physical fitness and overall health and wellness. Includes activity that is part of daily living and routine, structured exercise, and exercise prescribed as part of a medical treatment or recovery plan.

schemaClasses.name: PhysicalActivityCategory
schemaClasses.label: PhysicalActivityCategory
schemaClasses.description: Categories of physical activity, organized by physiologic classification.

schemaClasses.name: PhysicalExam
schemaClasses.label: PhysicalExam
schemaClasses.description: A type of physical examination of a patient performed by a physician.

schemaClasses.name: PhysicalTherapy
schemaClasses.label: PhysicalTherapy
schemaClasses.description: A process of progressive physical care and rehabilitation aimed at improving a health condition.

schemaClasses.name: Physician
schemaClasses.label: Physician
schemaClasses.description: An individual physician or a physician's office considered as a [[MedicalOrganization]].

schemaClasses.name: PhysiciansOffice
schemaClasses.label: PhysiciansOffice
schemaClasses.description: A doctor's office or clinic.

schemaClasses.name: Place
schemaClasses.label: Place
schemaClasses.description: Entities that have a somewhat fixed, physical extension.

schemaClasses.name: PlaceOfWorship
schemaClasses.label: PlaceOfWorship
schemaClasses.description: Place of worship, such as a church, synagogue, or mosque.

schemaClasses.name: PlanAction
schemaClasses.label: PlanAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of planning the execution of an event/task/action/reservation/plan to a future date.

schemaClasses.name: Play
schemaClasses.label: Play
schemaClasses.description: A play is a form of literature, usually consisting of dialogue between characters, intended for theatrical performance rather than just reading. Note: A performance of a Play would be a [[TheaterEvent]] or [[BroadcastEvent]] - the *Play* being the [[workPerformed]].

schemaClasses.name: PlayAction
schemaClasses.label: PlayAction
schemaClasses.description:
The act of playing/exercising/training/performing for enjoyment, leisure, recreation, competition or exercise.

Related actions:

* [[ListenAction]]: Unlike ListenAction (which is under ConsumeAction), PlayAction refers to performing for an audience or at an event, rather than consuming music.
* [[WatchAction]]: Unlike WatchAction (which is under ConsumeAction), PlayAction refers to showing/displaying for an audience or at an event, rather than consuming visual content.

schemaClasses.name: PlayGameAction
schemaClasses.label: PlayGameAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of playing a video game.

schemaClasses.name: Playground
schemaClasses.label: Playground
schemaClasses.description: A playground.

schemaClasses.name: Plumber
schemaClasses.label: Plumber
schemaClasses.description: A plumbing service.

schemaClasses.name: PodcastEpisode
schemaClasses.label: PodcastEpisode
schemaClasses.description: A single episode of a podcast series.

schemaClasses.name: PodcastSeason
schemaClasses.label: PodcastSeason
schemaClasses.description: A single season of a podcast. Many podcasts do not break down into separate seasons. In that case, PodcastSeries should be used.

schemaClasses.name: PodcastSeries
schemaClasses.label: PodcastSeries
schemaClasses.description: A podcast is an episodic series of digital audio or video files which a user can download and listen to.

schemaClasses.name: PoliceStation
schemaClasses.label: PoliceStation
schemaClasses.description: A police station.

schemaClasses.name: PoliticalParty
schemaClasses.label: PoliticalParty
schemaClasses.description: Organization: Political Party.

schemaClasses.name: Pond
schemaClasses.label: Pond
schemaClasses.description: A pond.

schemaClasses.name: PostOffice
schemaClasses.label: PostOffice
schemaClasses.description: A post office.

schemaClasses.name: PostalAddress
schemaClasses.label: PostalAddress
schemaClasses.description: The mailing address.

schemaClasses.name: PostalCodeRangeSpecification
schemaClasses.label: PostalCodeRangeSpecification
schemaClasses.description: Indicates a range of postal codes, usually defined as the set of valid codes between [[postalCodeBegin]] and [[postalCodeEnd]], inclusively.

schemaClasses.name: Poster
schemaClasses.label: Poster
schemaClasses.description: A large, usually printed placard, bill, or announcement, often illustrated, that is posted to advertise or publicize something.

schemaClasses.name: PreOrderAction
schemaClasses.label: PreOrderAction
schemaClasses.description: An agent orders a (not yet released) object/product/service to be delivered/sent.

schemaClasses.name: PrependAction
schemaClasses.label: PrependAction
schemaClasses.description: The act of inserting at the beginning if an ordered collection.

schemaClasses.name: Preschool
schemaClasses.label: Preschool
schemaClasses.description: A preschool.

schemaClasses.name: PresentationDigitalDocument
schemaClasses.label: PresentationDigitalDocument
schemaClasses.description: A file containing slides or used for a presentation.

schemaClasses.name: PreventionIndication
schemaClasses.label: PreventionIndication
schemaClasses.description: An indication for preventing an underlying condition, symptom, etc.

schemaClasses.name: PriceComponentTypeEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: PriceComponentTypeEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Enumerates different price components that together make up the total price for an offered product.

schemaClasses.name: PriceSpecification
schemaClasses.label: PriceSpecification
schemaClasses.description: A structured value representing a price or price range. Typically, only the subclasses of this type are used for markup. It is recommended to use [[MonetaryAmount]] to describe independent amounts of money such as a salary, credit card limits, etc.

schemaClasses.name: PriceTypeEnumeration
schemaClasses.label: PriceTypeEnumeration
schemaClasses.description: Enumerates different price types, for example list price, invoice price, and sale price.

schemaClasses.name: Product
schemaClasses.label: Product
schemaClasses.description: Any offered product or service. For example: a pair of shoes; a concert ticket; the rental of a car; a haircut; or an episode of a TV show streamed online.

schemaClasses.name: ProductCollection
schemaClasses.label: ProductCollection
schemaClasses.description: A set of products (either [[ProductGroup]]s or specific variants) that are listed together e.g. in an [[Offer]].

schemaClasses.name: ProductGroup
schemaClasses.label: ProductGroup
schemaClasses.description:
A ProductGroup represents a group of [[Product]]s that vary only in certain well-described ways, such as by [[size]], [[color]], [[material]] etc.

While a ProductGroup itself is not directly offered for sale, the various varying products that it represents can be. The ProductGroup serves as a prototype or template, standing in for all of the products who have an [[isVariantOf]] relationship to it. As such, properties (including additional types) can be applied to the ProductGroup to represent characteristics shared by each of the (possibly very many) variants. Properties that reference a ProductGroup are not included in this mechanism; neither are the following specific properties [[variesBy]], [[hasVariant]], [[url]].

schemaClasses.name: ProductModel
schemaClasses.label: ProductModel
schemaClasses.description: A datasheet or vendor specification of a product (in the sense of a prototypical description).

schemaClasses.name: ProfessionalService
schemaClasses.label: ProfessionalService
schemaClasses.description:
Original definition: "provider of professional services."\n\nThe general [[ProfessionalService]] type for local businesses was deprecated due to confusion with [[Service]]. For reference, the types that it included were: [[Dentist]],
        [[AccountingService]], [[Attorney]], [[Notary]], as well as types for several kinds of [[HomeAndConstructionBusiness]]: [[Electrician]], [[GeneralContractor]],
        [[HousePainter]], [[Locksmith]], [[Plumber]], [[RoofingContractor]]. [[LegalService]] was introduced as a more inclusive supertype of [[Attorney]].

schemaClasses.name: ProfilePage
schemaClasses.label: ProfilePage
schemaClasses.description: Web page type: Profile page.

schemaClasses.name: ProgramMembership
schemaClasses.label: ProgramMembership
schemaClasses.description: Used to describe membership in a loyalty programs (e.g. "StarAliance"), traveler clubs (e.g. "AAA"), purchase clubs ("Safeway Club"), etc.

schemaClasses.name: Project
schemaClasses.label: Project
schemaClasses.description:
An enterprise (potentially individual but typically collaborative), planned to achieve a particular aim.
Use properties from [[Organization]], [[subOrganization]]/[[parentOrganization]] to indicate project sub-structures.

schemaClasses.name: PronounceableText
schemaClasses.label: PronounceableText
schemaClasses.description: Data type: PronounceableText.

schemaClasses.name: Property
schemaClasses.label: Property
schemaClasses.description: A property, used to indicate attributes and relationships of some Thing; equivalent to rdf:Property.

schemaClasses.name: PropertyValue
schemaClasses.label: PropertyValue
schemaClasses.description: A property-value pair, e.g. representing a feature of a product or place. Use the 'name' property for the name of the property. If there is an additional human-readable version of the value, put that into the 'description' property.\n\n Always use specific schema.org properties when a) they exist and b) you can populate them. Using PropertyValue as a substitute will typically not trigger the same effect as using the original, specific property.

schemaClasses.name: PropertyValueSpecification
schemaClasses.label: PropertyValueSpecification
schemaClasses.description: A Property value specification.

schemaClasses.name: Protein
schemaClasses.label: Protein
schemaClasses.description: Protein is here used in its widest possible definition, as classes of amino acid based molecules. Amyloid-beta Protein in human (UniProt P05067), eukaryota (e.g. an OrthoDB group) or even a single molecule that one can point to are all of type :Protein. A protein can thus be a subclass of another protein, e.g. :Protein as a UniProt record can have multiple isoforms inside it which would also be :Protein. They can be imagined, synthetic, hypothetical or naturally occurring.

schemaClasses.name: PsychologicalTreatment
schemaClasses.label: PsychologicalTreatment
schemaClasses.description: A process of care relying upon counseling, dialogue and communication  aimed at improving a mental health condition without use of drugs.

schemaClasses.name: PublicSwimmingPool
schemaClasses.label: PublicSwimmingPool
schemaClasses.description: A public swimming pool.

schemaClasses.name: PublicToilet
schemaClasses.label: PublicToilet
schemaClasses.description: A public toilet is a room or small building containing one or more toilets (and possibly also urinals) which is available for use by the general public, or by customers or employees of certain businesses.

schemaClasses.name: PublicationEvent
schemaClasses.label: PublicationEvent
